title: Tư vấn chuyển việc

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (先輩)

question.jepang: 最近(さいきん)、転職(てんしょく)を考(かんが)えているって聞(き)いたけど、本当(ほんとう)ですか？
question.romaji: Saikin, tenshoku wo kangaete iru tte kiita kedo, hontou desu ka?
question.arti: Nghe nói bạn đang suy nghĩ về việc chuyển công việc — is that true?

choices.jepang: いいえ、今(いま)の仕事(しごと)がとても楽(たの)しいので、ずっとここにいたいです。
choices.romaji: Iie, ima no shigoto ga totemo tanoshii node, zutto koko ni itai desu.
choices.arti: No, công việc hiện tại rất vui nên tôi muốn ở đây mãi.

choices.jepang: はい、現状(げんじょう)を踏(ふ)まえて、新(あたら)しい環境(かんきょう)に挑戦(ちょうせん)したいと思(おも)っています。
choices.romaji: Hai, genjou wo fumaete, atarashii kankyou ni chousen shitai to omotteimasu.
choices.arti: Yes, cân nhắc tình hình hiện tại, tôi muốn thử thách môi trường mới.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Yes, cân nhắc tình hình hiện tại, tôi muốn thử thách môi trường mới' vì directly answers the question about changing jobs using ~を踏まえて, which cho thấy a well-considered decision. Trong khi đó, 'No, công việc hiện tại rất vui nên tôi muốn ở đây mãi' mâu thuẫn với the news already heard, preventing the conversation from continuing.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (先輩)

question.jepang: なるほど。転職(てんしょく)に伴(ともな)い、収入(しゅうにゅう)が下(さ)がる可能性(かのうせい)もありますよね。その点(てん)はどう考(かんが)えていますか？
question.romaji: Naruhodo. Tenshoku ni tomonai, shuunyuu ga sagaru kanousei mo arimasu yo ne. Sono ten wa dou kangaete imasu ka?
question.arti: I see. Cùng với việc chuyển công việc, có khả năng thu nhập có thể giảm. Bạn nghĩ sao về điều đó?

choices.jepang: 収入(しゅうにゅう)が下(さ)がりかねないとわかっていますが、長期的(ちょうきてき)なキャリアを考(かんが)えると、今(いま)動(うご)かざるを得(え)ません。
choices.romaji: Shuunyuu ga sagarikanenai to wakatte imasu ga, choukiteki na kyaria wo kangaeru to, ima ugokazaru wo emasen.
choices.arti: Tôi biết thu nhập có thể giảm, nhưng nghĩ đến sự nghiệp dài hạn, tôi không thể không hành động ngay bây giờ.

choices.jepang: 収入(しゅうにゅう)が下(さ)がっても、やりがいのある仕事(しごと)を選(えら)ぶべきだと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Shuunyuu ga sagatte mo, yarigai no aru shigoto wo erabu beki da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Dù lương giảm, tôi nghĩ tốt hơn nên chọn công việc có ý nghĩa.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Tôi biết thu nhập có thể giảm, nhưng nghĩ đến sự nghiệp dài hạn, tôi không thể không hành động ngay bây giờ' vì thừa nhận the risk with ~かねない and cho thấy determination with ~ざるを得ない — a realistic and mature consideration. Trong khi đó, 'Dù lương giảm, tôi nghĩ tốt hơn nên chọn công việc có ý nghĩa' is too idealistic mà không considering concrete risks.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: 先輩(せんぱい)、正直(しょうじき)に言(い)うと、今(いま)の会社(かいしゃ)の将来性(しょうらいせい)に不安(ふあん)を感(かん)じつつあります。
question.romaji: Senpai, shoujiki ni iu to, ima no kaisha no shourai-sei ni fuan wo kanjitsutsarimasu.
question.arti: Senpai, honestly, I'm becoming more and more worried about the future of the current company.

choices.jepang: それは深刻(しんこく)ですね。具体的(ぐたいてき)にどんな点(てん)が気(き)になっていますか？
choices.romaji: Sore wa shinkoku desu ne. Gutaiteki ni donna ten ga ki ni natte imasu ka?
choices.arti: That's serious. Specifically, what aspects are you worried about?

choices.jepang: そんなこと気(き)にしなくていいですよ。どこの会社(かいしゃ)も同(おな)じですから。
choices.romaji: Sonna koto ki ni shinakute ii desu yo. Doko no kaisha mo onaji desu kara.
choices.arti: Don't worry about it. All companies are the same anyway.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'That's serious. Specifically, what aspects are you worried about?' vì responds to the concern seriously and encourages deeper discussion — an appropriate attitude from a senior. Trong khi đó, 'Don't worry about it. All companies are the same anyway' belittles the other person's feelings and shuts down the conversation.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: 新規(しんき)事業(じぎょう)が次々(つぎつぎ)と失敗(しっぱい)しているにもかかわらず、経営陣(けいえいじん)は方針(ほうしん)を変(か)えようとしないんです。
question.romaji: Shinki jigyou ga tsugitsugi to shippai shite iru ni mo kakawarazu, keieijin wa houshin wo kaeyou to shinai n desu.
question.arti: New businesses keep failing one after another, but management won't change direction at all.

choices.jepang: 経営陣(けいえいじん)のことは気(き)にしなくていいですよ。自分(じぶん)の仕事(しごと)だけやっていればいいんです。
choices.romaji: Keieijin no koto wa ki ni shinakute ii desu yo. Jibun no shigoto dake yatte ireba ii n desu.
choices.arti: Don't worry about management. Just focus on your own work.

choices.jepang: それは確(たし)かに問題(もんだい)ですね。失敗(しっぱい)が続(つづ)いているにもかかわらず方針(ほうしん)を変(か)えないのは、会社(かいしゃ)の成長(せいちょう)を妨(さまた)げかねませんね。
choices.romaji: Sore wa tashika ni mondai desu ne. Shippai ga tsuzuite iru ni mo kakawarazu houshin wo kaenai no wa, kaisha no seichou wo samatage kanemasen ne.
choices.arti: That is indeed a problem. Refusing to change direction despite continuous failures could hinder the company's growth.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'That is indeed a problem. Refusing to change direction despite continuous failures could hinder the company's growth' vì sử dụng ~にもかかわらず and ~かねない để phân tích the situation logically. Trong khi đó, 'Don't worry about management. Just focus on your own work' bỏ qua valid concerns about the company's direction.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (先輩)

question.jepang: 業績(ぎょうせき)が伸(の)びている一方(いっぽう)で、社員(しゃいん)の離職率(りしょくりつ)も高(たか)まっているとのことですが、それでも転職(てんしょく)を急(いそ)ぐつもりですか？
question.romaji: Gyouseki ga nobite iru ippou de, shain no rishokuritsu mo takamatte iru to no koto desu ga, soredemo tenshoku wo isogu tsumori desu ka?
question.arti: The company's performance is rising, but on the other hand, the employee turnover rate is also high — even so, do you want to rush to change jobs?

choices.jepang: いいえ、状況(じょうきょう)が改善(かいぜん)される次第(しだい)、もう少(すこ)し様子(ようす)を見(み)るつもりです。
choices.romaji: Iie, joukyou ga kaizen sareru shidai, mou sukoshi yousu wo miru tsumori desu.
choices.arti: No, once the situation improves, I plan to wait a bit longer.

choices.jepang: はい、できるだけ早(はや)く辞(や)めたいです。もう限界(げんかい)です。
choices.romaji: Hai, dekiru dake hayaku yametai desu. Mou genkai desu.
choices.arti: Yes, I want to resign as soon as possible. I've reached my limit.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'No, once the situation improves, I plan to wait a bit longer' vì sử dụng ~次第 để cho thấy a mature attitude of not rushing and being willing to wait for the right conditions. Trong khi đó, 'Yes, I want to resign as soon as possible. I've reached my limit' seems emotional and unwise in the context of career consultation.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: 転職先(てんしょくさき)の業界(ぎょうかい)は決(き)まっていますか？IT業界(ぎょうかい)をはじめ、いろいろな選択肢(せんたくし)がありますよね。
question.romaji: Tenshoku-saki no gyoukai wa kimatte imasu ka? IT gyoukai wo hajime, iroirona sentakushi ga arimasu yo ne.
question.arti: Have you decided on the industry you want to move to? Starting with IT, there are many options, right?

choices.jepang: IT業界(ぎょうかい)をはじめ、コンサルティングやフィンテック分野(ぶんや)も視野(しや)に入(い)れつつあります。
choices.romaji: IT gyoukai wo hajime, konsarutingu ya fintekku bunya mo shiya ni iretsutsu arimasu.
choices.arti: Starting with IT, I'm also beginning to consider the consulting and fintech fields.

choices.jepang: まだ何(なに)も考(かんが)えていません。とりあえず辞(や)めてから考(かんが)えます。
choices.romaji: Mada nani mo kangaete imasen. Toriaezu yamete kara kangaemasu.
choices.arti: Haven't thought about anything yet. The important thing is to resign first and think later.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Starting with IT, I'm also beginning to consider the consulting and fintech fields' vì sử dụng ~をはじめ để chỉ ra several options and ~つつある for an ongoing process. Trong khi đó, 'Haven't thought about anything yet. The important thing is to resign first and think later' cho thấy unwise lack of preparation.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (先輩)

question.jepang: 先輩(せんぱい)の経験(けいけん)を踏(ふ)まえて、転職活動(てんしょくかつどう)で気(き)をつけるべきことを教(おし)えていただけますか？
question.romaji: Senpai no keiken wo fumaete, tenshoku katsudou de ki wo tsukeru beki koto wo oshiete itadakemasu ka?
question.arti: Based on your experience, senpai, could you tell me what to watch out for when job hunting?

choices.jepang: 転職(てんしょく)の理由(りゆう)を明確(めいかく)にしておかないわけにはいきません。面接(めんせつ)で必(かなら)ず聞(き)かれますから。
choices.romaji: Tenshoku no riyuu wo meikaku ni shite okanai wake ni wa ikimasen. Mensetsu de kanarazu kikaremasu kara.
choices.arti: You absolutely must clarify your reason for changing jobs. You'll definitely be asked during the interview.

choices.jepang: 給料(きゅうりょう)が高(たか)ければどこでもいいと思(おも)いますよ。あとは何(なに)も考(かんが)えなくて大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)です。
choices.romaji: Kyuuryou ga takakereba doko demo ii to omoimasu yo. Ato wa nani mo kangaenakute daijoubu desu.
choices.arti: If the salary is high, anywhere is fine by me. No need to think about anything else.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'You absolutely must clarify your reason for changing jobs. You'll definitely be asked during the interview' vì sử dụng ~わけにはいかない để nhấn mạnh something important that cannot be ignored. Trong khi đó, 'If the salary is high, anywhere is fine' is too shallow and doesn't show serious preparation for a job change.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (先輩)

question.jepang: それから、転職(てんしょく)エージェントを使(つか)うかどうかも考(かんが)えた方(ほう)がいいよ。自分(じぶん)だけで探(さが)すのは限界(げんかい)があるからね。
question.romaji: Sorekara, tenshoku eejento wo tsukau ka dou ka mo kangaeta hou ga ii yo. Jibun dake de sagasu no wa genkai ga aru kara ne.
question.arti: Also, you need to think about whether to use a job change agent or not. Searching on your own has its limits.

choices.jepang: エージェントに頼(たよ)らなくても、自分(じぶん)で全部(ぜんぶ)できると思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Eejento ni tayoranakute mo, jibun de zenbu dekiru to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Even mà không relying on an agent, I can do everything by myself.

choices.jepang: 確(たし)かにそうですね。非公開(ひこうかい)求人(きゅうじん)もあるものの、エージェント選(えら)びも慎重(しんちょう)にしなければなりませんね。
choices.romaji: Tashika ni sou desu ne. Hikoukai kyuujin mo aru mono no, eejento erabi mo shinchou ni shinakereba narimasen ne.
choices.arti: That's true. Although there are non-public job openings, choosing an agent also requires caution.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'That's true. Although there are non-public job openings, choosing an agent also requires caution' vì sử dụng ~ものの để thừa nhận the advantage while thể hiện caution. Trong khi đó, 'Even mà không relying on an agent, I can do everything by myself' is quá tự tin and bỏ qua valid advice from a senior.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: 面接(めんせつ)では、前(まえ)の会社(かいしゃ)の悪口(わるぐち)を言(い)ってしまいがちですが、それは避(さ)けた方(ほう)がいいですよね？
question.romaji: Mensetsu de wa, mae no kaisha no waruguchi wo itte shimai gachi desu ga, sore wa saketa hou ga ii desu yo ne?
question.arti: During interviews, people often end up badmouthing their previous company, but it's better to avoid that, right?

choices.jepang: 正直(しょうじき)に全部(ぜんぶ)話(はな)した方(ほう)がいいですよ。嘘(うそ)をつくのはよくないですから。
choices.romaji: Shoujiki ni zenbu hanashita hou ga ii desu yo. Uso wo tsuku no wa yoku nai desu kara.
choices.arti: It's better to tell everything honestly. Lying is not good.

choices.jepang: 前(まえ)の会社(かいしゃ)が悪(わる)いからといって、面接(めんせつ)で悪口(わるぐち)を言(い)うのは印象(いんしょう)が悪(わる)くなりかねません。ポジティブな理由(りゆう)に言(い)い換(か)えましょう。
choices.romaji: Mae no kaisha ga warui kara to itte, mensetsu de waruguchi wo iu no wa inshou ga waruku nari kanemasen. Pojitibu na riyuu ni iikaemashou.
choices.arti: Even if the previous company was bad, badmouthing it in an interview can create a bad impression. Replace it with positive reasons.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Even if the previous company was bad, badmouthing it in an interview can create a bad impression. Replace it with positive reasons' vì sử dụng ~からといって để cho thấy that a reason alone isn't enough to justify an action, and ~かねない để cảnh báo about the risk. Trong khi đó, 'It's better to tell everything honestly' bỏ qua important communication strategy in interviews.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (先輩)

question.jepang: それから、今(いま)の職場(しょくば)に不満(ふまん)があるにもかかわらず、退職(たいしょく)まで誠実(せいじつ)に働(はたら)くことが大切(たいせつ)ですよ。
question.romaji: Sorekara, ima no shokuba ni fuman ga aru ni mo kakawarazu, taishoku made seijitsu ni hataraku koto ga taisetsu desu yo.
question.arti: Also, even though there's dissatisfaction at your current workplace, it's very important to keep working seriously until you resign.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。最後(さいご)まで責任(せきにん)を持(も)って仕事(しごと)をするつもりです。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Saigo made sekinin wo motte shigoto wo suru tsumori desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely right, senpai. I intend to work with full responsibility until the end.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。不満(ふまん)があるにもかかわらず、プロとして最後(さいご)まで全力(ぜんりょく)を尽(つ)くします。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Fuman ga aru ni mo kakawarazu, puro toshite saigo made zenryoku wo tsukushimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. Despite the dissatisfaction, I will give my best until the end as a chuyên nghiệp.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Despite the dissatisfaction, I will give my best until the end as a chuyên nghiệp' vì actively uses ~にもかかわらず and cho thấy a high level of chuyên nghiệp awareness. Trong khi đó, 'Absolutely right, senpai. I intend to work with full responsibility until the end' is polite but doesn't use the target grammar and lacks deep understanding.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: 先輩(せんぱい)、今日(きょう)は本当(ほんとう)にありがとうございました。先輩(せんぱい)のアドバイスに基(もと)づいて、しっかり準備(じゅんび)して転職活動(てんしょくかつどう)を進(すす)めます。
question.romaji: Senpai, kyou wa hontou ni arigatou gozaimashita. Senpai no adobaisu ni motozuite, shikkari junbi shite tenshoku katsudou wo susumemasu.
question.arti: Senpai, thank you so much for today. Based on your advice, I will prepare seriously and continue the job change process.

choices.jepang: 頑張(がんば)ってね。何(なに)かあったらいつでも相談(そうだん)してください。いい結果(けっか)が出(で)ることを祈(いの)っています。
choices.romaji: Ganbatte ne. Nanika attara itsu demo soudan shite kudasai. Ii kekka ga deru koto wo inotte imasu.
choices.arti: Good luck. If anything comes up, consult me anytime. I hope it goes well.

choices.jepang: 応援(おうえん)しているよ。転職(てんしょく)は人生(じんせい)の大(おお)きな決断(けつだん)だからこそ、慎重(しんちょう)に、でも前向(まえむ)きに進(すす)んでほしい。困(こま)ったことがあれば、遠慮(えんりょ)なく連絡(れんらく)してね。
choices.romaji: Ouen shite iru yo. Tenshoku wa jinsei no ooki na ketsudan dakara koso, shinchou ni, demo maemuki ni susunde hoshii. Komatta koto ga areba, enryo naku renraku shite ne.
choices.arti: I support you. Precisely because changing jobs is a big life decision, I hope you proceed carefully but positively. If you have any problems, don't hesitate to reach out.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Precisely because changing jobs is a big life decision, I hope you proceed carefully but positively' vì sử dụng ~だからこそ để nhấn mạnh tầm quan trọng của being serious, cung cấp a warm and meaningful conclusion. Trong khi đó, 'Good luck. If anything comes up, consult me anytime' is nice but quá ngắn gọn and lacks depth as a closing for an important consultation.

title: Chuẩn bị sống ở nước ngoài

conversation.speaker: Suzuki (友人)

question.jepang: 来月(らいげつ)からドイツに赴任(ふにん)するって本当(ほんとう)？準備(じゅんび)は進(すす)んでいる？
question.romaji: Raigetsu kara Doitsu ni funin suru tte hontou? Junbi wa susunde iru?
question.arti: Is it true you're being posted to Germany next month? Is the preparation going well?

choices.jepang: まだ全然(ぜんぜん)準備(じゅんび)していないよ。なんとかなるでしょ。
choices.romaji: Mada zenzen junbi shite inai yo. Nantoka naru desho.
choices.arti: Haven't done anything at all. It'll work out somehow.

choices.jepang: うん、海外生活(かいがいせいかつ)に伴(ともな)う手続(てつづ)きが多(おお)くて、毎日(まいにち)バタバタしているよ。
choices.romaji: Un, kaigai seikatsu ni tomonau tetsuzuki ga ookute, mainichi batabata shite iru yo.
choices.arti: Yes, there's so much paperwork that comes with living abroad, I'm busy every day.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'There's so much paperwork that comes with living abroad, I'm busy every day' vì sử dụng ~に伴う để giải thích a realistic situation and answers the question about readiness. Trong khi đó, 'Haven't done anything at all. It'll work out somehow' cho thấy an thiếu trách nhiệm attitude for an overseas transfer.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki (友人)

question.jepang: 言語(げんご)の壁(かべ)はどうするの？ドイツ語(ご)はできるの？
question.romaji: Gengo no kabe wa dou suru no? Doitsugo wa dekiru no?
question.arti: What about the language barrier? Can you speak German?

choices.jepang: ドイツ語(ご)は少(すこ)しずつ上達(じょうたつ)しつつあるけど、まだまだ不安(ふあん)だよ。
choices.romaji: Doitsugo wa sukoshi zutsu joutatsu shitsutsu aru kedo, madamada fuan da yo.
choices.arti: My German is gradually improving, but I'm still worried.

choices.jepang: ドイツ語(ご)の習得(しゅうとく)が追(お)いつかず、英語(えいご)で乗(の)り切(き)らざるを得(え)ない状況(じょうきょう)です。
choices.romaji: Doitsugo no shuutoku ga oitsukazu, eigo de norikirarazaru wo enai joukyou desu.
choices.arti: My German isn't sufficient yet, so I have no choice but to get by with English.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'My German isn't sufficient yet, so I have no choice but to get by with English' vì sử dụng ~ざるを得ない để thể hiện a realistic necessity. Trong khi đó, 'My German is gradually improving, but I'm still worried' uses ~つつある but doesn't answer the question about the strategy for overcoming the language barrier.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (本人)

question.jepang: 現地(げんち)の生活費(せいかつひ)は高(たか)いと聞(き)いていますが、給与(きゅうよ)の面(めん)での補償(ほしょう)はありますか？
question.romaji: Genchi no seikatsuhi wa takai to kiite imasu ga, kyuuyo no men de no hoshou wa arimasu ka?
question.arti: I heard living costs are high there. Is there salary compensation?

choices.jepang: わかりません。会社(かいしゃ)に聞(き)いてみます。
choices.romaji: Wakarimasen. Kaisha ni kiite mimasu.
choices.arti: I don't know. Try asking the company.

choices.jepang: 海外赴任(かいがいふにん)に伴(ともな)い、手当(てあて)が支給(しきゅう)される予定(よてい)です。詳細(しょうさい)は届(とど)き次第(しだい)お知(し)らせします。
choices.romaji: Kaigai funin ni tomonai, teate ga shikyuu sareru yotei desu. Shousai wa todoki shidai oshirase shimasu.
choices.arti: Along with the overseas assignment, an allowance will be provided. I'll let you know the details once the information arrives.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Along with the overseas assignment, an allowance will be provided. I'll let you know the details once the information arrives' vì tự nhiên combines ~に伴い and ~次第 in one sentence, cung cấp complete information. Trong khi đó, 'I don't know. Try asking the company' is quá ngắn gọn and uninformative for someone who should be preparing.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki (友人)

question.jepang: 家族(かぞく)も一緒(いっしょ)に連(つ)れて行(い)くの？それとも単身赴任(たんしんふにん)？
question.romaji: Kazoku mo issho ni tsurete iku no? Soretomo tanshin funin?
question.arti: Is your family coming with you? Or are you going alone?

choices.jepang: 子供(こども)の学校(がっこう)のことを考(かんが)えると、家族(かぞく)を連(つ)れて行(い)かないわけにはいかないんだよね。
choices.romaji: Kodomo no gakkou no koto wo kangaeru to, kazoku wo tsurete ikanai wake ni wa ikanai n da yo ne.
choices.arti: Thinking about the children's schooling, there's no way I can not bring my family.

choices.jepang: 子供(こども)の教育(きょういく)のことを踏(ふ)まえて、今回(こんかい)は単身(たんしん)で行(い)くことにしました。
choices.romaji: Kodomo no kyouiku no koto wo fumaete, konkai wa tanshin de iku koto ni shimashita.
choices.arti: Considering the children's education, I've decided to go alone this time.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Considering the children's education, I've decided to go alone this time' vì sử dụng ~を踏まえて để cho thấy a decision made after careful consideration. Trong khi đó, 'Thinking about the children's schooling, there's no way I can not bring my family' is contradictory — if thinking about the children's education, one should also consider that transferring schools abroad is also difficult for children.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (本人)

question.jepang: 単身赴任(たんしんふにん)だと、家族(かぞく)との距離(きょり)が遠(とお)くなる一方(いっぽう)で、自分(じぶん)の時間(じかん)は増(ふ)えるかもしれないね。
question.romaji: Tanshin funin da to, kazoku to no kyori ga tooku naru ippou de, jibun no jikan wa fueru kamo shirenai ne.
question.arti: If you go alone, on one hand the distance from family increases, but on the other hand you might have more time for yourself.

choices.jepang: 家族(かぞく)のことは考(かんが)えないようにしています。仕事(しごと)に集中(しゅうちゅう)するだけです。
choices.romaji: Kazoku no koto wa kangaenai you ni shite imasu. Shigoto ni shuuchuu suru dake desu.
choices.arti: I try not to think about family. I'll just focus on work.

choices.jepang: そうだね。寂(さび)しいものの、オンラインで毎日(まいにち)連絡(れんらく)を取(と)れるから、なんとかなると思(おも)う。
choices.romaji: Sou da ne. Sabishii mono no, onrain de mainichi renraku wo toreru kara, nantoka naru to omou.
choices.arti: That's true. Although it'll be lonely, since we can contact each other online every day, I'm sure we can get through it.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Although it'll be lonely, since we can contact each other online every day, I'm sure we can get through it' vì sử dụng ~ものの để thừa nhận the negative side trong khi vẫn optimistic with a concrete solution. Trong khi đó, 'I try not to think about family. I'll just focus on work' cho thấy an unhealthy avoidance attitude.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki (友人)

question.jepang: ドイツの文化(ぶんか)や習慣(しゅうかん)については、もう調(しら)べた？
question.romaji: Doitsu no bunka ya shuukan ni tsuite wa, mou shirabeta?
question.arti: Have you looked into German culture and customs?

choices.jepang: うん、ドイツの文化(ぶんか)に基(もと)づいたビジネスマナーを勉強(べんきょう)しているよ。時間(じかん)に厳(きび)しい文化(ぶんか)らしいから、遅刻(ちこく)は絶対(ぜったい)にできないんだって。
choices.romaji: Un, Doitsu no bunka ni motozuita bijinesu manaa wo benkyou shite iru yo. Jikan ni kibishii bunka rashii kara, chikoku wa zettai ni dekinai n datte.
choices.arti: Yes, I'm studying business etiquette based on German culture. I heard they're strict about time, so being late is absolutely not allowed.

choices.jepang: まだです。行(い)ってから覚(おぼ)えればいいと思(おも)っています。
choices.romaji: Mada desu. Itte kara oboereba ii to omotte imasu.
choices.arti: Not yet. I'll learn about it once I get there.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I'm studying business etiquette based on German culture' vì sử dụng ~に基づいた phù hợp và cho thấy serious preparation. Trong khi đó, 'Not yet. I'll learn about it once I get there' cho thấy lack of preparation that could cause serious problems when living abroad.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (本人)

question.jepang: 海外(かいがい)での孤独感(こどくかん)が心配(しんぱい)です。うまくやっていけるかどうか自信(じしん)がなくて…
question.romaji: Kaigai de no kodokukan ga shinpai desu. Umaku yatte ikeru ka dou ka jishin ga nakute...
question.arti: I'm worried about loneliness abroad. I'm not confident whether I can endure it or not...

choices.jepang: 孤独感(こどくかん)を感(かん)じかねないけど、現地(げんち)のコミュニティに積極的(せっきょくてき)に参加(さんか)することで乗(の)り越(こ)えられると思(おも)うよ。
choices.romaji: Kodokukan wo kanjikanenai kedo, genchi no komyuniti ni sekkyokuteki ni sanka suru koto de norikoeru to omou yo.
choices.arti: It's true you might feel lonely, but by actively joining local communities, you can surely get through it.

choices.jepang: 大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)だよ！絶対(ぜったい)楽(たの)しいから心配(しんぱい)しないで！
choices.romaji: Daijoubu da yo! Zettai tanoshii kara shinpai shinaide!
choices.arti: You'll be fine! It'll definitely be fun, don't worry!

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'It's true you might feel lonely, but by actively joining local communities, you can surely get through it' vì sử dụng ~かねない để thừa nhận rủi ro của loneliness, then offers a concrete solution. Trong khi đó, 'You'll be fine! It'll definitely be fun, don't worry!' xem nhẹ the other person's feelings mà không offering useful advice.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki (友人)

question.jepang: ドイツで病気(びょうき)になった場合(ばあい)はどうするの？保険(ほけん)とかは大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)？
question.romaji: Doitsu de byouki ni natta baai wa dou suru no? Hoken toka wa daijoubu?
question.arti: What if you get sick in Germany? Are insurance and such taken care of?

choices.jepang: 海外(かいがい)赴任(ふにん)に伴(ともな)い、会社(かいしゃ)が海外旅行(かいがいりょこう)保険(ほけん)に加入(かにゅう)してくれる予定(よてい)なので、医療費(いりょうひ)の心配(しんぱい)はないはずです。
choices.romaji: Kaigai funin ni tomonai, kaisha ga kaigai ryokou hoken ni kanyuu shite kureru yotei na node, iryouhi no shinpai wa nai hazu desu.
choices.arti: Along with the overseas assignment, the company plans to enroll me in overseas travel insurance, so medical costs shouldn't be a concern.

choices.jepang: 保険(ほけん)のことはまだ調(しら)べていないから、わからないな。
choices.romaji: Hoken no koto wa mada shirabete inai kara, wakaranai na.
choices.arti: I haven't looked into insurance, so I don't know.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Along with the overseas assignment, the company plans to enroll me in overseas travel insurance' vì sử dụng ~に伴い phù hợp và cho thấy good preparation. Trong khi đó, 'I haven't looked into insurance' cho thấy serious negligence because health insurance is essential for living abroad.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (本人)

question.jepang: 赴任(ふにん)期間(きかん)はどのくらいの予定(よてい)なの？いつか日本(にほん)に戻(もど)ってくるんでしょ？
question.romaji: Funin kikan wa dono kurai no yotei na no? Itsuka Nihon ni modotte kuru n desho?
question.arti: How long is the planned assignment? You'll come back to Japan eventually, right?

choices.jepang: 最初(さいしょ)は3年(ねん)の予定(よてい)だけど、現地(げんち)での成果(せいか)次第(しだい)で延長(えんちょう)もあり得(え)ると言(い)われています。
choices.romaji: Saisho wa sannen no yotei da kedo, genchi de no seika shidai de enchou mo arieru to iwarete imasu.
choices.arti: Initially it's planned for 3 years, but depending on the results there, an extension could also happen.

choices.jepang: わからない。ずっと向(む)こうにいるかもしれない。
choices.romaji: Wakaranai. Zutto mukou ni iru kamo shirenai.
choices.arti: I don't know. Maybe I'll stay there permanently.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Initially it's planned for 3 years, but depending on the results there, an extension could also happen' vì sử dụng ~次第で phù hợp để chỉ ra the determining variable. Trong khi đó, 'I don't know. Maybe I'll stay there permanently' doesn't provide clear information and cho thấy indifference toward future plans.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki (友人)

question.jepang: 海外(かいがい)に住(す)むのは大変(たいへん)な一方(いっぽう)で、貴重(きちょう)な経験(けいけん)にもなるよね。楽(たの)しみにしている？
question.romaji: Kaigai ni sumu no wa taihen na ippou de, kichou na keiken ni mo naru yo ne. Tanoshimi ni shite iru?
question.arti: Living abroad is tough, but on the other hand it becomes a valuable experience. Are you excited?

choices.jepang: 不安(ふあん)はあるにもかかわらず、新(あたら)しい文化(ぶんか)に触(ふ)れられることをとても楽(たの)しみにしています。
choices.romaji: Fuan wa aru ni mo kakawarazu, atarashii bunka ni fureru koto wo totemo tanoshimi ni shite imasu.
choices.arti: Despite the worries, I very much look forward to the opportunity to experience a new culture.

choices.jepang: もちろんです。大変(たいへん)な一方(いっぽう)で、自分(じぶん)を成長(せいちょう)させるチャンスだと前向(まえむ)きに捉(とら)えています。
choices.romaji: Mochiron desu. Taihen na ippou de, jibun wo seichou saseru chansu da to maemuki ni toraete imasu.
choices.arti: Of course. Although it's tough, I view it positively as a chance to grow.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Despite the worries, I very much look forward to the opportunity to experience a new culture' vì sử dụng ~にもかかわらず để cho thấy an honest contrast between worry and enthusiasm. Trong khi đó, 'Although it's tough, I view it positively as a chance to grow' is fine but uses ~一方で which was already used in the question, rather than the more appropriate target grammar.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (本人)

question.jepang: 出発(しゅっぱつ)の日(ひ)が近(ちか)づくにつれて、やるべきことが山積(やまづ)みだよ。送別会(そうべつかい)もあるし、引(ひ)っ越(こ)しの準備(じゅんび)もあるし。
question.romaji: Shuppatsu no hi ga chikazuku ni tsurete, yaru beki koto ga yamazumi da yo. Soubetsukai mo aru shi, hikkoshi no junbi mo aru shi.
question.arti: As the departure date gets closer, more and more things pile up. There are farewell parties, moving preparations too.

choices.jepang: 全部(ぜんぶ)やらなくても大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)だよ。向(む)こうに行(い)ってからやればいい。
choices.romaji: Zenbu yaranakute mo daijoubu da yo. Mukou ni itte kara yareba ii.
choices.arti: You don't have to do everything. Just handle it over there.

choices.jepang: 大変(たいへん)だけど、出発(しゅっぱつ)までにやるべきことをリスト化(か)して、一(ひと)つずつ片(かた)づけていくしかないね。手伝(てつだ)えることがあったら言(い)ってね。
choices.romaji: Taihen da kedo, shuppatsu made ni yaru beki koto wo risuto-ka shite, hitotsu zutsu katazukete iku shika nai ne. Tetsudaeru koto ga attara itte ne.
choices.arti: It is tough, but there's no choice but to make a list of things to do before leaving and finish them one by one. If there's anything I can help with, let me know.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'It is tough, but there's no choice but to make a list and finish them one by one. If there's anything I can help with, let me know' vì offers practical advice and concrete support as a friend. Trong khi đó, 'You don't have to do everything. Just handle it over there' gives thiếu trách nhiệm advice for important preparations.

title: Chọn bảo hiểm

conversation.speaker: Satou (保険代理店員)

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)はどのような保険(ほけん)についてご相談(そうだん)でしょうか？
question.romaji: Honjitsu wa donna you na hoken ni tsuite go-soudan deshou ka?
question.arti: What kind of insurance would you like to consult about today?

choices.jepang: 結婚(けっこん)に伴(ともな)い、生命保険(せいめいほけん)の見直(みなお)しを考(かんが)えています。
choices.romaji: Kekkon ni tomonai, seimei hoken no minaoushi wo kangaete imasu.
choices.arti: Along with getting married, I'm thinking about reviewing my life insurance.

choices.jepang: 保険(ほけん)のことはよくわからないので、適当(てきとう)に選(えら)んでください。
choices.romaji: Hoken no koto wa yoku wakaranai node, tekitou ni erande kudasai.
choices.arti: I don't understand insurance. Just pick whatever for me.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Along with getting married, I'm thinking about reviewing my life insurance' vì sử dụng ~に伴い to tự nhiên connect a life change (marriage) with the action needed. Trong khi đó, 'I don't understand insurance. Just pick whatever for me' cho thấy a passive attitude không phù hợp for financial consultation.

conversation.speaker: Satou (保険代理店員)

question.jepang: 現在(げんざい)の収入(しゅうにゅう)や家族(かぞく)構成(こうせい)を踏(ふ)まえて、最適(さいてき)なプランをご提案(ていあん)することができます。
question.romaji: Genzai no shuunyuu ya kazoku kousei wo fumaete, saiteki na puran wo go-teian suru koto ga dekimasu.
question.arti: Based on your current income and family structure, we can offer the most suitable plan.

choices.jepang: はい、ぜひお願(ねが)いします。子供(こども)が生(う)まれる予定(よてい)もあり、保障(ほしょう)を厚(あつ)くしないわけにはいかないと感(かん)じています。
choices.romaji: Hai, zehi onegai shimasu. Kodomo ga umareru yotei mo ari, hoshyou wo atsuku shinai wake ni wa ikanai to kanjite imasu.
choices.arti: Yes, please. Since we're also planning to have children, I feel I must strengthen the coverage.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。では、現状(げんじょう)を詳(くわ)しくお伝(つた)えします。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Dewa, genjou wo kuwashiku otsutae shimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. In that case, I'll explain my current situation in detail.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Since we're also planning to have children, I feel I must strengthen the coverage' vì sử dụng ~わけにはいかない để thể hiện situational obligation and cung cấp strong context. Trong khi đó, 'Thank you. In that case, I'll explain my current situation in detail' is polite but doesn't use N2 grammar and lacks the urgency of insurance needs.

conversation.speaker: Yamada (本人)

question.jepang: 掛(か)け捨(す)て型(がた)と貯蓄(ちょちく)型(がた)、どちらがいいでしょうか？
question.romaji: Kakesute-gata to chochiku-gata, dochira ga ii deshou ka?
question.arti: Between term insurance and savings-type insurance, which is better?

choices.jepang: どちらも一長一短(いちちょういったん)ですが、今後(こんご)の資産形成(しさんけいせい)を考(かんが)えると、貯蓄(ちょちく)型(がた)が有利(ゆうり)な一方(いっぽう)で、保険料(ほけんりょう)が高(たか)くなりがちです。
choices.romaji: Dochira mo icchouitttan desu ga, kongo no shisan keisei wo kangaeru to, chochiku-gata ga yuuri na ippou de, hokenryou ga takaku nari gachi desu.
choices.arti: Both have advantages and disadvantages, but thinking about future asset building, savings-type insurance is beneficial; on the other hand, premiums tend to be more expensive.

choices.jepang: 貯蓄型(ちょちくがた)の方(ほう)が絶対(ぜったい)いいです。みんなそれを選(えら)んでいます。
choices.romaji: Chochiku-gata no hou ga zettai ii desu. Minna sore wo erande imasu.
choices.arti: Savings-type insurance is definitely better. Everyone chooses that.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Both have advantages and disadvantages, but thinking about future asset building, savings-type insurance is beneficial; on the other hand, premiums tend to be more expensive' vì sử dụng ~一方で để đưa ra a cân bằng, chuyên nghiệp explanation. Trong khi đó, 'Savings-type insurance is definitely better. Everyone chooses that' is too generalized and không chuyên nghiệp as financial advice.

conversation.speaker: Satou (保険代理店員)

question.jepang: 医療保険(いりょうほけん)も合(あ)わせてご検討(けんとう)されますか？最近(さいきん)、医療費(いりょうひ)が増加(ぞうか)しつつあります。
question.romaji: Iryou hoken mo awasete go-kentou saremasu ka? Saikin, iryouhi ga zouka shitsutsu arimasu.
question.arti: Would you also like to consider health insurance? Recently, medical costs have been continuously rising.

choices.jepang: そうですね。入院(にゅういん)や手術(しゅじゅつ)の費用(ひよう)を考(かんが)えると、医療保険(いりょうほけん)も検討(けんとう)せざるを得(え)ませんね。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ne. Nyuuin ya shujutsu no hiyou wo kangaeru to, iryou hoken mo kentou serazaru wo emasen ne.
choices.arti: That's right. Thinking about hospitalization and surgery costs, I really must consider health insurance too.

choices.jepang: はい、医療費(いりょうひ)が上(あ)がりつつある現状(げんじょう)を踏(ふ)まえると、備(そな)えておくべきだと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Hai, iryouhi ga agari tsutsu aru genjou wo fumaete, sonaete oku beki da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Yes, considering the fact that medical costs continue to rise, I think preparation is necessary.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Considering the fact that medical costs continue to rise, I think preparation is necessary' vì tự nhiên combines ~つつある and ~を踏まえて. Trong khi đó, 'Thinking about hospitalization and surgery costs, I really must consider health insurance too' also contains N2 grammar but less directly responds to the rising medical cost trend mentioned by the agent.

conversation.speaker: Yamada (本人)

question.jepang: がん保険(ほけん)についてはどうでしょうか？最近(さいきん)、がんになる人(ひと)が増(ふ)えていると聞(き)きますが。
question.romaji: Gan hoken ni tsuite wa dou deshou ka? Saikin, gan ni naru hito ga fuete iru to kikimasu ga.
question.arti: What about cancer insurance? Recently, I hear more and more people are getting cancer.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)り、がんの罹患率(りかんりつ)が高(たか)まりつつある現在(げんざい)、若(わか)いうちから備(そな)えておくことに越(こ)したことはございません。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori, gan no rikanritsu ga takamari tsutsu aru genzai, wakai uchi kara sonaete oku koto ni koshita koto wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: Exactly as you say. As cancer rates continue to rise, it wouldn't hurt to prepare from a young age.

choices.jepang: がんは怖(こわ)いけど、若(わか)いうちはなりませんから大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)ですよ。
choices.romaji: Gan wa kowai kedo, wakai uchi wa narimasen kara daijoubu desu yo.
choices.arti: Cancer is scary, but you won't get it while you're young.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'As cancer rates continue to rise, it wouldn't hurt to prepare from a young age' vì sử dụng ~つつある phù hợp và gives chuyên nghiệp advice based on data. Trong khi đó, 'While you're young, you won't get it' is medically inaccurate and không chuyên nghiệp as advice from an insurance agent.

conversation.speaker: Yamada (本人)

question.jepang: 保険料(ほけんりょう)の支払(しはら)いが家計(かけい)を圧迫(あっぱく)しかねないと心配(しんぱい)しています。
question.romaji: Hokenryou no shiharai ga kakei wo appaku shikanenai to shinpai shite imasu.
question.arti: I'm worried that premium payments might burden my family's finances.

choices.jepang: 保険(ほけん)はとても大切(たいせつ)なので、家計(かけい)が苦(くる)しくなっても払(はら)うべきです。
choices.romaji: Hoken wa totemo taisetsu na node, kakei ga kurushiku natte mo harau beki desu.
choices.arti: Insurance is very important, so even if family finances are tight, you must keep paying.

choices.jepang: ご心配(ごしんぱい)はごもっともです。収入(しゅうにゅう)の10%以内(いない)に保険料(ほけんりょう)を抑(おさ)えることをお勧(すす)めします。
choices.romaji: Go-shinpai wa go-mottomo desu. Shuunyuu no juppaa-seinto inai ni hokenryou wo osaeru koto wo osusume shimasu.
choices.arti: Your concern is very reasonable. I recommend keeping premiums under 10% of your income.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Your concern is very reasonable. I recommend keeping premiums under 10% of your income' vì addresses the client's concern with a concrete and chuyên nghiệp solution. Trong khi đó, 'Insurance is very important, so even if family finances are tight, you must keep paying' bỏ qua the client's concern and gives không thực tế advice.

conversation.speaker: Satou (保険代理店員)

question.jepang: ご契約(けいやく)にあたって、特約(とくやく)を付(つ)けるかどうかもご検討(けんとう)ください。先進(せんしん)医療(いりょう)特約(とくやく)をはじめ、様々(さまざま)なオプションがございます。
question.romaji: Go-keiyaku ni atatte, tokuyaku wo tsukeru ka dou ka mo go-kentou kudasai. Senshin iryou tokuyaku wo hajime, samazama na opushon ga gozaimasu.
question.arti: In the contract, please also consider whether to add riders. Starting with advanced treatment riders, there are various options.

choices.jepang: 必要(ひつよう)な特約(とくやく)だけを選(えら)びたいのですが、家族(かぞく)の状況(じょうきょう)に基(もと)づいて、どの特約(とくやく)が優先(ゆうせん)されるべきか教(おし)えていただけますか？
choices.romaji: Hitsuyou na tokuyaku dake wo erabitai no desu ga, kazoku no joukyou ni motozuite, dono tokuyaku ga yuusen sareru beki ka oshiete itadakemasu ka?
choices.arti: I'd like to select only the necessary riders. Based on my family situation, could you tell me which riders should be prioritized?

choices.jepang: 特約(とくやく)は全部(ぜんぶ)つけてください。多(おお)い方(ほう)が安心(あんしん)です。
choices.romaji: Tokuyaku wa zenbu tsukete kudasai. Ooi hou ga anshin desu.
choices.arti: Just add all the riders. The more the better for peace of mind.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I'd like to select only the necessary riders. Based on my family situation, could you tell me which ones should be prioritized?' vì sử dụng ~に基づいて phù hợp và cho thấy a wise approach. Trong khi đó, 'Just add all the riders' would unnecessarily increase premiums.

conversation.speaker: Yamada (本人)

question.jepang: ネットの保険(ほけん)と比(くら)べて、対面(たいめん)で契約(けいやく)するメリットは何(なん)でしょうか？
question.romaji: Netto no hoken to kurabete, taimen de keiyaku suru meritto wa nan deshou ka?
question.arti: Compared to online insurance, what are the advantages of face-to-face contracts?

choices.jepang: ネット保険(ほけん)は信用(しんよう)できませんから、絶対(ぜったい)に対面(たいめん)にしてください。
choices.romaji: Netto hoken wa shinyou dekimasen kara, zettai ni taimen ni shite kudasai.
choices.arti: Online insurance can't be trusted, so it has to be face-to-face.

choices.jepang: ネット保険(ほけん)は安(やす)い一方(いっぽう)で、万(まん)が一(いち)のときにきめ細(こま)かい対応(たいおう)が受(う)けられない場合(ばあい)がございます。対面(たいめん)では担当者(たんとうしゃ)がお客様(きゃくさま)の状況(じょうきょう)に合(あ)わせてサポートいたします。
choices.romaji: Netto hoken wa yasui ippou de, man ga ichi no toki ni kime komakai taiou ga ukerarenai baai ga gozaimasu. Taimen de wa tantousha ga okyakusama no joukyou ni awasete sapooto itashimasu.
choices.arti: Online insurance is indeed cheap, but on the other hand, in emergencies you might not get detailed service. With face-to-face, an agent will support you according to your situation.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Online insurance is indeed cheap, but on the other hand, in emergencies you might not get detailed service' vì sử dụng ~一方で for a cân bằng and chuyên nghiệp comparison. Trong khi đó, 'Online insurance can't be trusted' unfairly disparages competitors mà không clear basis.

conversation.speaker: Satou (保険代理店員)

question.jepang: では、お客様(おきゃくさま)のご状況(じょうきょう)を踏(ふ)まえた最適(さいてき)プランをご用意(ようい)しますので、資料(しりょう)が届(とど)き次第(しだい)ご連絡(れんらく)いたします。
question.romaji: Dewa, o-kyaku-sama no go-joukyou wo fumaeta saiteki puran wo go-youi shimasu node, shiryou ga todoki shidai go-renraku itashimasu.
question.arti: In that case, we'll prepare the best plan based on your situation, and we'll contact you once the documents are ready.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。ご連絡(れんらく)をお待(ま)ちしております。保険料(ほけんりょう)の詳細(しょうさい)が確認(かくにん)でき次第(しだい)、家族(かぞく)とも相談(そうだん)したいと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Go-renraku wo omachi shite orimasu. Hokenryou no shousai ga kakunin deki shidai, kazoku to mo soudan shitai to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. I'll wait for your contact. Once the premium details can be confirmed, I'd also like to discuss with my family.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。連絡(れんらく)をいただき次第(しだい)、すぐに確認(かくにん)いたします。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Renraku wo itadaki shidai, sugu ni kakunin itashimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. Once you contact me, I'll check right away.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I'll wait for your contact. Once the premium details can be confirmed, I'd also like to discuss with my family' vì sử dụng ~次第 tự nhiên và cho thấy a responsible decision-making process. Trong khi đó, 'Once you contact me, I'll check right away' also uses ~次第 but doesn't show mature consideration like involving family.

conversation.speaker: Yamada (本人)

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)は丁寧(ていねい)にご説明(せつめい)いただき、ありがとうございました。保険(ほけん)の大切(たいせつ)さがよくわかりました。
question.romaji: Honjitsu wa teinei ni go-setsumei itadaki, arigatou gozaimashita. Hoken no taisetsusa ga yoku wakarimashita.
question.arti: Thank you for explaining in detail today. I now understand how important insurance is.

choices.jepang: 早(はや)く契約(けいやく)してくださいね。今月中(こんげつちゅう)がキャンペーン期間(きかん)ですから。
choices.romaji: Hayaku keiyaku shite kudasai ne. Kongetsu-chuu ga kyanpeen kikan desu kara.
choices.arti: Please sign the contract quickly. This month is still the campaign period.

choices.jepang: こちらこそありがとうございます。ご家族(かぞく)の将来(しょうらい)を守(まも)るためにも、保険(ほけん)選(えら)びは慎重(しんちょう)にされるべきですからね。何(なに)かご不明(ふめい)な点(てん)がございましたら、いつでもご連絡(れんらく)ください。
choices.romaji: Kochira koso arigatou gozaimasu. Go-kazoku no shourai wo mamoru tame ni mo, hoken erabi wa shinchou ni sareru beki desu kara ne. Nanika go-fumei na ten ga gozaimashitara, itsu demo go-renraku kudasai.
choices.arti: Thank you from our side as well. Since it's about protecting your family's future, insurance selection must indeed be done carefully. If anything is unclear, please don't hesitate to contact us anytime.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Thank you from our side as well. Insurance selection must indeed be done carefully. If anything is unclear, please don't hesitate to contact us anytime' vì cung cấp a chuyên nghiệp closing that nhấn mạnh careful selection. Trong khi đó, 'Please sign the contract quickly. This month is still the campaign period' seems pushy and prioritizes sales over the client's interest.

title: Thảo luận vấn đề môi trường

conversation.speaker: Kimura (教授)

question.jepang: 地球温暖化(ちきゅうおんだんか)の問題(もんだい)について、皆(みな)さんはどのようにお考(かんが)えですか？
question.romaji: Chikyuu ondanka no mondai ni tsuite, mina-san wa dono you ni o-kangae desu ka?
question.arti: What are your views on the issue of global warming?

choices.jepang: 気温(きおん)が上昇(じょうしょう)しつつある現状(げんじょう)を踏(ふ)まえると、早急(そうきゅう)な対策(たいさく)が必要(ひつよう)だと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Kion ga joushou shitsutsu aru genjou wo fumaete, soukyuu na taisaku ga hitsuyou da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Considering the fact that temperatures continue to rise, I believe immediate action is needed.

choices.jepang: 温暖化(おんだんか)は自然(しぜん)なことだから、特(とく)に心配(しんぱい)する必要(ひつよう)はないと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Ondanka wa shizen na koto dakara, toku ni shinpai suru hitsuyou wa nai to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Global warming is a natural phenomenon, so there's no need to worry too much.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Considering the fact that temperatures continue to rise, I believe immediate action is needed' vì combines ~つつある and ~を踏まえて để xây dựng a strong data-based argument. Trong khi đó, 'Global warming is a natural phenomenon, so there's no need to worry too much' bỏ qua scientific facts and is không phù hợp in an academic discussion.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (学生)

question.jepang: 経済発展(けいざいはってん)と環境保護(かんきょうほご)は両立(りょうりつ)できるのでしょうか？
question.romaji: Keizai hatten to kankyou hogo wa ryouritsu dekiru no deshou ka?
question.arti: Can economic development and environmental protection go hand in hand?

choices.jepang: 難(むずか)しい問題(もんだい)ですね。経済(けいざい)が成長(せいちょう)する一方(いっぽう)で、環境(かんきょう)への負担(ふたん)も増(ふ)えかねないというジレンマがあります。
choices.romaji: Muzukashii mondai desu ne. Keizai ga seichou suru ippou de, kankyou he no futan mo fue kanai to iu jirenma ga arimasu.
choices.arti: That's indeed a difficult issue. There's a dilemma that economic growth can also lead to increased environmental burden.

choices.jepang: グリーン技術(ぎじゅつ)の発展(はってん)に伴(ともな)い、両立(りょうりつ)の可能性(かのうせい)は高(たか)まりつつあると思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Guriin gijutsu no hatten ni tomonai, ryouritsu no kanousei wa takamari tsutsu aru to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Along with the development of green technology, I believe the possibility of coexistence is increasing.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Along with the development of green technology, the possibility of coexistence is increasing' vì sử dụng ~に伴い and ~つつある để mô tả a positive fact-based technological trend. Trong khi đó, 'There's a dilemma that economic growth can lead to increased environmental burden' is logical but only states the problem mà không offering a solution or hope.

conversation.speaker: Kimura (教授)

question.jepang: 個人(こじん)レベルでの環境(かんきょう)への取(と)り組(く)みについて、どのようにお考(かんが)えですか？
question.romaji: Kojin reberu de no kankyou he no torikumi ni tsuite, dono you ni o-kangae desu ka?
question.arti: What do you think about environmental conservation efforts at the individual level?

choices.jepang: 個人(こじん)ではなく、政府(せいふ)が全部(ぜんぶ)やるべきです。私(わたし)たちには関係(かんけい)ありません。
choices.romaji: Kojin de wa naku, seifu ga zenbu yaru beki desu. Watashitachi ni wa kankei arimasen.
choices.arti: It's not individuals; the government should do everything. It's not our concern.

choices.jepang: 個人(こじん)の努力(どりょく)だけでは限界(げんかい)があるにもかかわらず、一人(ひとり)ひとりが行動(こうどう)を変(か)えることは重要(じゅうよう)だと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Kojin no doryoku dake de wa genkai ga aru ni mo kakawarazu, hitorihitori ga koudou wo kaeru koto wa juuyou da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Although individual efforts alone have their limits, I think it's important for each person to change their behavior.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Although individual efforts alone have their limits, I think it's important for each person to change their behavior' vì sử dụng ~にもかかわらず để thừa nhận limitations while still affirming giá trị của action. Trong khi đó, 'It's not individuals; the government should do everything. It's not our concern' rejects individual responsibility and is không phù hợp in an academic discussion.

conversation.speaker: Watanabe (学生)

question.jepang: 再生(さいせい)可能(かのう)エネルギーへの移行(いこう)は重要(じゅうよう)ですが、コストが高(たか)いという問題(もんだい)がありますよね。
question.romaji: Saisei kanou enerugii he no ikou wa juuyou desu ga, kosuto ga takai to iu mondai ga arimasu yo ne.
question.arti: Transitioning to renewable energy is important, but there's the problem of high costs.

choices.jepang: 移行(いこう)コストは高(たか)い一方(いっぽう)で、長期的(ちょうきてき)には化石(かせき)燃料(ねんりょう)に頼(たよ)り続(つづ)けるよりコストが低(ひく)くなる可能性(かのうせい)があります。
choices.romaji: Ikou kosuto wa takai ippou de, choukiteki ni wa kaseki nenryou ni tayori tsuzukeru yori kosuto ga hikuku naru kanousei ga arimasu.
choices.arti: On one hand transition costs are indeed high, but in the long run there's a possibility it could be cheaper than continuing to rely on fossil fuels.

choices.jepang: コスト面(めん)は課題(かだい)ですが、化石(かせき)燃料(ねんりょう)を使(つか)い続(つづ)けると環境(かんきょう)に取(と)り返(かえ)しのつかないダメージを与(あた)えかねません。
choices.romaji: Kosuto men wa kadai desu ga, kaseki nenryou wo tsukai tsuzukeru to kankyou ni torikaeshi no tsukanai dameeji wo ataekane masen.
choices.arti: Cost is certainly a challenge, but continuing to use fossil fuels could cause permanent environmental damage.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'On one hand transition costs are indeed high, but in the long run there's a possibility it could be cheaper than continuing to rely on fossil fuels' vì sử dụng ~一方で để cung cấp a cân bằng long-term perspective. Trong khi đó, 'Continuing to use fossil fuels could cause permanent environmental damage' is true but only focuses on risk mà không addressing the cost issue raised.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (学生)

question.jepang: 企業(きぎょう)のESG投資(とうし)が増(ふ)えていると聞(き)きましたが、これは環境(かんきょう)問題(もんだい)の解決(かいけつ)に役(やく)に立(た)つのでしょうか？
question.romaji: Kigyou no ESG toushi ga fuete iru to kikimashita ga, kore wa kankyou mondai no kaiketsu ni yaku ni tatsu no deshou ka?
question.arti: I hear that corporate ESG investment is increasing. Is this beneficial for solving environmental problems?

choices.jepang: ESG投資(とうし)は利益(りえき)を追求(ついきゅう)する一方(いっぽう)で、社会的(しゃかいてき)責任(せきにん)も果(は)たすという考(かんが)え方(かた)に基(もと)づいています。環境(かんきょう)改善(かいぜん)に貢献(こうけん)し得(う)る仕組(しく)みだと言(い)えます。
choices.romaji: ESG toushi wa rieki wo tsuikyuu suru ippou de, shakaiteki sekinin mo hatasu to iu kangaekata ni motozuite imasu. Kankyou kaizen ni kouken shiuru shikumi da to iemasu.
choices.arti: ESG investment is based on the idea that while pursuing profit, it also fulfills social responsibility. It can be said to be a mechanism with potential to contribute to environmental improvement.

choices.jepang: ESG投資(とうし)は企業(きぎょう)のイメージアップのためだけで、環境(かんきょう)には何(なに)の影響(えいきょう)もありません。
choices.romaji: ESG toushi wa kigyou no imeeji appu no tame dake de, kankyou ni wa nani no eikyou mo arimasen.
choices.arti: ESG investment is just for improving corporate image and has no impact on the environment at all.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'ESG investment is based on the idea that while pursuing profit, it also fulfills social responsibility' vì sử dụng ~に基づいて and ~一方で for a cân bằng analysis. Trong khi đó, 'ESG investment is just for improving corporate image' oversimplifies and bỏ qua the real impact of ESG.

conversation.speaker: Kimura (教授)

question.jepang: 国際的(こくさいてき)な環境(かんきょう)協定(きょうてい)に日本(にほん)はもっと積極的(せっきょくてき)に参加(さんか)すべきでしょうか？
question.romaji: Kokusaiteki na kankyou kyoutei ni Nihon wa motto sekkyokuteki ni sanka suru beki deshou ka?
question.arti: Should Japan participate more actively in international environmental agreements?

choices.jepang: 地球規模(ちきゅうきぼ)の問題(もんだい)である以上(いじょう)、日本(にほん)も積極的(せっきょくてき)に参加(さんか)しないわけにはいかないと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Chikyuu kibo no mondai de aru ijou, Nihon mo sekkyokuteki ni sanka shinai wake ni wa ikanai to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Because this is a global-scale problem, I believe Japan cannot avoid participating actively.

choices.jepang: 日本(にほん)は小(ちい)さな国(くに)なので、国際(こくさい)協定(きょうてい)に参加(さんか)しなくてもいいと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Nihon wa chiisana kuni na node, kokusai kyoutei ni sanka shinakute mo ii to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Japan is a small country, so there's no need to participate in international agreements.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Because this is a global-scale problem, Japan cannot avoid participating actively' vì sử dụng ~わけにはいかない để xây dựng a logical argument based on the scale of the problem. Trong khi đó, 'Japan is a small country, so there's no need to participate in international agreements' is factually incorrect and bỏ qua Japan's global responsibility.

conversation.speaker: Watanabe (学生)

question.jepang: プラスチック削減(さくげん)の取(と)り組(く)みが世界中(せかいじゅう)で広(ひろ)がりつつありますが、日本(にほん)の対応(たいおう)は十分(じゅうぶん)だと思(おも)いますか？
question.romaji: Purasuchikku sakugen no torikumi ga sekaijuu de hirogari tsutsu arimasu ga, Nihon no taiou wa juubun da to omoimasu ka?
question.arti: Plastic reduction efforts are spreading worldwide. Do you think Japan's response has been sufficient?

choices.jepang: レジ袋(ぶくろ)有料化(ゆうりょうか)は進(すす)んだものの、使(つか)い捨(す)てプラスチック全体(ぜんたい)の削減(さくげん)にはまだ課題(かだい)が多(おお)いと感(かん)じます。
choices.romaji: Reji bukuro yuuryou-ka wa susunda mono no, tsukaisute purasuchikku zentai no sakugen ni wa mada kadai ga ooi to kanjimasu.
choices.arti: Although the implementation of paid plastic bags has progressed, I think there are still many challenges in overall single-use plastic reduction.

choices.jepang: 日本(にほん)は十分(じゅうぶん)やっています。これ以上(いじょう)やる必要(ひつよう)はありません。
choices.romaji: Nihon wa juubun yatte imasu. Kore ijou yaru hitsuyou wa arimasen.
choices.arti: Japan has done enough. No need for more.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Although the implementation of paid plastic bags has progressed, there are still many challenges in overall single-use plastic reduction' vì sử dụng ~ものの để thừa nhận progress đồng thời cũng pointing out remaining shortcomings. Trong khi đó, 'Japan has done enough' is too complacent and bỏ qua the fact that Japan is still one of the largest plastic waste producers.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (学生)

question.jepang: では、私(わたし)たち学生(がくせい)にできることは何(なん)でしょうか？
question.romaji: Dewa, watashitachi gakusei ni dekiru koto wa nan deshou ka?
question.arti: Then, what can we do as students?

choices.jepang: 今後(こんご)の環境(かんきょう)問題(もんだい)を踏(ふ)まえ、専門知識(せんもんちしき)を身(み)につけた上(うえ)で社会(しゃかい)に貢献(こうけん)するのが学生(がくせい)としての責任(せきにん)だと考(かんが)えます。
choices.romaji: Kongo no kankyou mondai wo fumaete, senmon chishiki wo mi ni tsuketa ue de shakai ni kouken suru no ga gakusei toshite no sekinin da to kangaemasu.
choices.arti: Considering future environmental issues, I think the responsibility of students is to contribute to society after mastering specialized knowledge.

choices.jepang: 勉強(べんきょう)するだけでなく、環境問題(かんきょうもんだい)への意識(いしき)を広(ひろ)めることも大切(たいせつ)だと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Benkyou suru dake de naku, kankyou mondai he no ishiki wo hiromeru koto mo taisetsu da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Not just studying, but raising awareness about environmental issues is also important.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Considering future environmental issues, the responsibility of students is to contribute to society after mastering specialized knowledge' vì sử dụng ~を踏まえ để xây dựng a specific future-oriented argument. Trong khi đó, 'Not just studying, but raising awareness about environmental issues is also important' is fine but lacks specificity about the student's role and doesn't use N2 grammar.

conversation.speaker: Kimura (教授)

question.jepang: 皆(みな)さんの意見(いけん)はとても参考(さんこう)になりました。環境問題(かんきょうもんだい)は複雑(ふくざつ)ですが、議論(ぎろん)を続(つづ)けることが大切(たいせつ)ですね。
question.romaji: Mina-san no iken wa totemo sankou ni narimashita. Kankyou mondai wa fukuzatsu desu ga, giron wo tsuzukeru koto ga taisetsu desu ne.
question.arti: Everyone's opinions have been very valuable. Environmental issues are indeed complex, but it's important to keep discussing, right?

choices.jepang: はい。問題(もんだい)が複雑(ふくざつ)であるからこそ、多角的(たかくてき)な視点(してん)で議論(ぎろん)を深(ふか)めていかなければならないと思(おも)います。本日(ほんじつ)はありがとうございました。
choices.romaji: Hai. Mondai ga fukuzatsu de aru kara koso, takakuteki na shiten de giron wo fukamete ikanakereba naranai to omoimasu. Honjitsu wa arigatou gozaimashita.
choices.arti: Yes. Precisely because the issues are complex, we must deepen discussion from various perspectives. Thank you for today.

choices.jepang: 議論(ぎろん)しても結局(けっきょく)何(なに)も変(か)わりません。行動(こうどう)あるのみです。
choices.romaji: Giron shite mo kekkyoku nani mo kawarimasen. Koudou aru nomi desu.
choices.arti: Discussion doesn't change anything in the end. What matters is action.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Precisely because the issues are complex, we must deepen discussion from various perspectives' vì sử dụng ~からこそ để nhấn mạnh giá trị của discussion and cung cấp a mang tính xây dựng closing. Trong khi đó, 'Discussion doesn't change anything in the end' belittles tầm quan trọng của academic dialogue in a classroom context.

question.arti: Everyone's opinions have been very valuable. Environmental issues are indeed complex, but it's important to keep discussing, right?
