choices.romaji: Giron shite mo kekkyoku nani mo kawarimasen. Koudou aru nomi desu.
choices.arti: Discussion doesn't change anything in the end. What matters is action.

choices.jepang: はい。問題(もんだい)が複雑(ふくざつ)であるからこそ、多角的(たかくてき)な視点(してん)で議論(ぎろん)を深(ふか)めていかなければならないと思(おも)います。本日(ほんじつ)はありがとうございました。
choices.romaji: Hai. Mondai ga fukuzatsu de aru kara koso, takakuteki na shiten de giron wo fukamete ikanakereba naranai to omoimasu. Honjitsu wa arigatou gozaimashita.
choices.arti: Yes. Precisely because the issues are complex, we must deepen discussion from various perspectives. Thank you for today.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là "Precisely because the issues are complex, we must deepen discussion from various perspectives" vì sử dụng ~からこそ để nhấn mạnh giá trị của discussion and cung cấp a mang tính xây dựng closing. Trong khi đó, "Discussion doesn't change anything in the end" belittles tầm quan trọng của academic dialogue in a classroom context.

title: Tham dự tang lễ

conversation.speaker: Nakamura (同僚)

question.jepang: 田中(たなか)部長(ぶちょう)のお父様(とうさま)がお亡(なく)くなりになったと聞(き)きました。お通夜(つや)には行(い)かれますか？
question.romaji: Tanaka buchou no o-tou-sama ga o-nakunari ni natta to kikimashita. O-tsuya ni wa ikaremasu ka?
question.arti: I heard Manager Tanaka's father passed away. Are you going to attend the tsuya?

choices.jepang: はい、お世話(せわ)になっている方(かた)のご家族(かぞく)のことですから、伺(うかが)わないわけにはいきません。
choices.romaji: Hai, o-sewa ni natte iru kata no go-kazoku no koto desu kara, ukagawanai wake ni wa ikimasen.
choices.arti: Yes, since this concerns the family of someone who has helped me a lot, there's no way I can not attend.

choices.jepang: 知(し)らない人(ひと)のことだし、行(い)かなくてもいいんじゃないですか。
choices.romaji: Shiranai hito no koto da shi, ikanakute mo ii n ja nai desu ka.
choices.arti: I don't know the person, so it's fine not to go.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Since this concerns the family of someone who has helped me a lot, there's no way I can not attend' vì sử dụng ~わけにはいかない để thể hiện a social obligation that cannot be ignored in Japanese culture. Trong khi đó, 'I don't know the person, so it's fine not to go' bỏ qua the relationship with the supervisor and Japanese social norms regarding funerals.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura (同僚)

question.jepang: ご香典(こうでん)はいくら包(つつ)めばいいでしょうか？
question.romaji: Go-kouden wa ikura tsutsumeба ii deshou ka?
question.arti: How much should be wrapped for condolence money?

choices.jepang: 部長(ぶちょう)との関係(かんけい)を踏(ふ)まえると、5000円(えん)以下(いか)では失礼(しつれい)になりかねませんので、1万円(いちまんえん)が適切(てきせつ)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Buchou to no kankei wo fumaete to, gosen en ika de wa shitsurei ni nari kanemasu node, ichiman en ga tekisetsu de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Considering the relationship with the Manager, anything under 5,000 yen could be considered rude, so 10,000 yen is more appropriate.

choices.jepang: 相場(そうば)は5000円(えん)から1万円(いちまんえん)ですが、お付(つ)き合(あ)いの深(ふか)さを踏(ふ)まえて金額(きんがく)を決(き)めるといいと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Souba wa gosen en kara ichiman en desu ga, o-tsukiai no fukasa wo fumaete kingaku wo kimeru to ii to omoimasu.
choices.arti: The range is 5,000 to 10,000 yen, but it's better to decide the amount based on closeness of the relationship.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Considering the relationship with the Manager, anything under 5,000 yen could be considered rude, so 10,000 yen is more appropriate' vì combines ~を踏まえると and ~かねない để đưa ra a concrete recommendation considering social risk. Trong khi đó, 'The range is 5,000 to 10,000 yen, but decide based on closeness' is too general and doesn't provide the specific amount needed.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: お葬式(そうしき)での服装(ふくそう)について教(おし)えていただけますか？
question.romaji: O-soushiki de no fukusou ni tsuite oshiete itadakemasu ka?
question.arti: Could you tell me about appropriate clothing for a funeral?

choices.jepang: 喪服(もふく)を着用(ちゃくよう)するのが基本(きほん)です。光(ひか)る装飾品(そうしょくひん)は避(さ)けざるを得(え)ない場合(ばあい)を除(のぞ)き、つけないようにしてください。
choices.romaji: Mofuku wo chakuyou suru no ga kihon desu. Hikaru soushokuhin wa sakeza ru wo enai baai wo nozoite, tsukenai you ni shite kudasai.
choices.arti: Wearing mourning clothes is the basic rule. Unless circumstances require otherwise, please avoid shiny jewelry.

choices.jepang: 黒(くろ)い服(ふく)なら何(なん)でもいいと思(おも)いますよ。あまり気(き)にしなくていいです。
choices.romaji: Kuroi fuku nara nan demo ii to omoimasu yo. Amari ki ni shinakute ii desu.
choices.arti: Any black clothes are fine. No need to pay too much attention.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Wearing mourning clothes is the basic rule. Unless circumstances require otherwise, please avoid shiny jewelry' vì sử dụng ~ざるを得ない in the context of a logical exception and cung cấp accurate guidance. Trong khi đó, 'Any black clothes are fine. No need to pay too much attention' is too dismissive of Japanese funeral dress codes which are carefully maintained.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura (同僚)

question.jepang: 香典袋(こうでんぶくろ)の表書(おもてが)きはどう書(か)けばいいですか？宗派(しゅうは)によって違(ちが)うんですよね？
question.romaji: Kouden bukuro no omotegaki wa dou kakeba ii desu ka? Shuuha ni yotte chigau n desu yo ne?
question.arti: How should the writing on the condolence envelope be done? It differs depending on the religious sect, right?

choices.jepang: 宗派(しゅうは)がわからない場合(ばあい)は、「ご霊前(れいぜん)」と書(か)くのが一般的(いっぱんてき)です。ただし、浄土真宗(じょうどしんしゅう)の場合(ばあい)は「ご仏前(ぶつぜん)」と書(か)かざるを得(え)ないので、事前(じぜん)に確認(かくにん)することをお勧(すす)めします。
choices.romaji: Shuuha ga wakaranai baai wa, 'go-reizen' to kaku no ga ippanteki desu. Tadashi, Jodo Shinshuu no baai wa 'go-butsuzen' to kakaza ru wo enai node, jizen ni kakunin suru koto wo osusume shimasu.
choices.arti: If you don't know the sect, writing 'Go-reizen' is common. But for Jodo Shinshu, you must write 'Go-butsuzen', so it's recommended to confirm beforehand.

choices.jepang: 何(なに)を書(か)いても同(おな)じですよ。気(き)にしなくて大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)です。
choices.romaji: Nani wo kaite mo onaji desu yo. Ki ni shinakute daijoubu desu.
choices.arti: Whatever you write is the same. No need to think about it.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'If you don't know the sect, writing Go-reizen is common. But for Jodo Shinshu, you must write Go-butsuzen' vì cung cấp accurate guidance and uses ~ざるを得ない để nhấn mạnh an important difference. Trong khi đó, 'Whatever you write is the same' cho thấy ignorance that could cause an embarrassing social mistake.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura (同僚)

question.jepang: 最近(さいきん)、家族葬(かぞくそう)が増(ふ)えつつありますが、そのような場合(ばあい)でも参列(さんれつ)してもいいのでしょうか？
question.romaji: Saikin, kazoku sou ga fue tsutsu arimasu ga, sono you na baai de mo sanretsu shite mo ii no deshou ka?
question.arti: Recently family funerals are increasing, but is it okay not to attend in that case?

choices.jepang: 家族葬(かぞくそう)が増(ふ)えつつある現代(げんだい)では、招待(しょうたい)なしに参列(さんれつ)するとご遺族(いぞく)に失礼(しつれい)になりかねないので、まず連絡(れんらく)するべきです。
choices.romaji: Kazoku sou ga fue tsutsu aru gendai de wa, shoutai nashi ni sanretsu suru to go-izoku ni shitsurei ni nari kanemasu node, mazu renraku suru beki desu.
choices.arti: In an era when family funerals are increasing, attending mà không being invited could be considered rude by the family, so it's better to contact them first.

choices.jepang: 家族葬(かぞくそう)の場合(ばあい)、ご遺族(いぞく)の意向(いこう)を踏(ふ)まえて、事前(じぜん)にご確認(かくにん)されることをお勧(すす)めします。
choices.romaji: Kazoku sou no baai, go-izoku no ikou wo fumaete, jizen ni go-kakunin suru koto wo osusume shimasu.
choices.arti: For family funerals, I recommend confirming based on the family's wishes first.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'In an era when family funerals are increasing, attending mà không being invited could be considered rude by the family, so it's better to contact them first' vì integrates ~つつある and ~かねない để xây dựng an argument based on social trends. Trong khi đó, 'I recommend confirming based on the family's wishes first' is correct but doesn't explain the reason behind tầm quan trọng của confirmation.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: お焼香(しょうこう)の作法(さほう)がわからなくて困(こま)っています。
question.romaji: O-shoukoo no sahou ga wakaranakute komatte imasu.
question.arti: I'm confused because I don't know the incense offering (shoukoo) etiquette.

choices.jepang: 難(むずか)しいことは気(き)にしないで、周(まわ)りの人(ひと)の真似(まね)をすればいいですよ。
choices.romaji: Muzukashii koto wa ki ni shinaide, mawari no hito no mane wo sureba ii desu yo.
choices.arti: Don't stress about the difficult parts, just follow what people around you do.

choices.jepang: 宗派(しゅうは)によって作法(さほう)が異(こと)なる一方(いっぽう)で、基本的(きほんてき)な流(なが)れは共通(きょうつう)していますので、事前(じぜん)に確認(かくにん)しておくといいですよ。
choices.romaji: Shuuha ni yotte sahou ga kotonaru ippou de, kihonteki na nagare wa kyoutsuu shite imasu node, jizen ni kakunin shite oku to ii desu yo.
choices.arti: The etiquette differs by sect, but on the other hand the basic flow is the same, so it's better to check beforehand.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'The etiquette differs by sect, but on the other hand the basic flow is the same, so it's better to check beforehand' vì sử dụng ~一方で để đề cập variations while affirming basic similarities with practical guidance. Trong khi đó, 'Don't stress about the difficult parts, just follow what people around you do' oversimplifies and doesn't respect important funeral etiquette in Japanese culture.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura (同僚)

question.jepang: お通夜(つや)の席(せき)で、故人(こじん)との思(おも)い出(で)を話(はな)すべきでしょうか？それとも控(ひか)えた方(ほう)がいいですか？
question.romaji: O-tsuya no seki de, kojin to no omoide wo hanasu beki deshou ka? Soretomo hikaeta hou ga ii desu ka?
question.arti: At the tsuya, should I share memories of the deceased? Or is it better to hold back?

choices.jepang: たくさん話(はな)した方(ほう)がいいですよ。ご遺族(いぞく)も喜(よろこ)びます。
choices.romaji: Takusan hanashita hou ga ii desu yo. Go-izoku mo yorokobimasu.
choices.arti: It's better to share a lot. The family would be happy too.

choices.jepang: 思(おも)い出(で)を語(かた)ることでご遺族(いぞく)が慰(なぐさ)められることもある一方(いっぽう)で、場(ば)の雰囲気(ふんいき)を読(よ)んで短(みじか)く控(ひか)えめにする方(ほう)が望(のぞ)ましいでしょう。
choices.romaji: Omoide wo kataru koto de go-izoku ga nagusamerareru koto mo aru ippou de, ba no fun'iki wo yonde mijikaku hikaeme ni suru hou ga nozomashii deshou.
choices.arti: On one hand sharing memories can comfort the grieving family, but it's better to read the atmosphere and share briefly and simply.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'On one hand sharing memories can comfort the grieving family, but it's better to read the atmosphere and share briefly and simply' vì sử dụng ~一方で for mature consideration. Trong khi đó, 'It's better to share a lot' is insensitive to the mourning atmosphere and could cause discomfort.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: 葬儀(そうぎ)が終(お)わった後(あと)、ご遺族(いぞく)にどのように声(こえ)をかければいいですか？
question.romaji: Sougi ga owatta ato, go-izoku ni dono you ni koe wo kakereba ii desu ka?
question.arti: After the ceremony, what words should be said to the bereaved family?

choices.jepang: 「この度(たび)はご愁傷様(しゅうしょうさま)でございます」と伝(つた)えるのが一般的(いっぱんてき)です。長(なが)い言葉(ことば)は避(さ)けた方(ほう)がいいでしょう。
choices.romaji: 「Konotabi wa go-shuushou-sama de gozaimasu」to tsutaeru no ga ippanteki desu. Nagai kotoba wa saketa hou ga ii deshou.
choices.arti: Saying 'Kono tabi wa go-shuushou-sama de gozaimasu' is standard. It's best to avoid lengthy words.

choices.jepang: ご遺族(いぞく)の気持(きも)ちを踏(ふ)まえ、「この度(たび)はご愁傷様(しゅうしょうさま)でございます」と短(みじか)く丁寧(ていねい)に伝(つた)えざるを得(え)ません。余計(よけい)なことは言(い)わない方(ほう)が賢明(けんめい)です。
choices.romaji: Go-izoku no kimochi wo fumaete, 「Konotabi wa go-shuushou-sama de gozaimasu」to mijikaku teinei ni tsutaeza ru wo emasen. Yokei na koto wa iwanai hou ga kenmei desu.
choices.arti: Considering the feelings of the bereaved family, one must offer 'Kono tabi wa go-shuushou-sama de gozaimasu' briefly and politely. It's wiser not to say unnecessary things.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Considering the feelings of the bereaved family, one must offer condolences briefly and politely' vì sử dụng ~を踏まえ and ~ざるを得ない để nhấn mạnh empathy and propriety. Trong khi đó, 'Saying the standard condolence is common. It's best to avoid lengthy words' is correct but doesn't use N2 grammar and lacks deep consideration.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura (同僚)

question.jepang: 葬儀(そうぎ)の後(あと)、会社(かいしゃ)で部長(ぶちょう)にお悔(く)やみを改(あらた)めて伝(つた)えた方(ほう)がいいですか？
question.romaji: Sougi no ato, kaisha de buchou ni o-kuyami wo aratamete tsutaeta hou ga ii desu ka?
question.arti: After the funeral, should condolences be expressed again to the manager at the office?

choices.jepang: 部長(ぶちょう)が復帰(ふっき)されたら、「大変(たいへん)でしたね。何(なに)かお手伝(てつだ)いできることがあれば、おっしゃってください」と声(こえ)をかけるのが自然(しぜん)でしょう。あまり長々(ながなが)と言(い)うべきではないですね。
choices.romaji: Buchou ga fukki saretara, 'Taihen deshita ne. Nanika otetsudai dekiru koto ga areba, osshatte kudasai' to koe wo kakeru no ga shizen deshou. Amari naganaga to iu beki de wa nai desu ne.
choices.arti: When the manager returns to work, it's appropriate to say 'It must have been tough. If there's anything I can help with, please let me know.' Don't speak too lengthy.

choices.jepang: 会社(かいしゃ)では何(なに)も言(い)わない方(ほう)がいいです。仕事(しごと)と私生活(しせいかつ)は別(べつ)ですから。
choices.romaji: Kaisha de wa nani mo iwanai hou ga ii desu. Shigoto to shiseikatsu wa betsu desu kara.
choices.arti: At the office it's better not to say anything. Work and private life are separate.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'When the manager returns to work, it's appropriate to say a brief condolence and offer help' vì cho thấy appropriate care in Japanese work culture. Trong khi đó, 'At the office it's better not to say anything' bỏ qua important interpersonal relationships in the workplace in Japan.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (本人)

question.jepang: いろいろ教(おし)えてくれてありがとう。日本(にほん)の葬儀(そうぎ)のマナーは複雑(ふくざつ)ですが、ちゃんと対応(たいおう)できそうです。
question.romaji: Iroiro oshiete kurete arigatou. Nihon no sougi no manaa wa fukuzatsu desu ga, chanto taiou dekisou desu.
question.arti: Thank you for teaching me so much. Japanese funeral etiquette is complex, but I think I can handle it well.

choices.jepang: もう大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)でしょ。簡単(かんたん)ですから。
choices.romaji: Mou daijoubu desho. Kantan desu kara.
choices.arti: You'll be fine. It's easy.

choices.jepang: どういたしまして。マナーが複雑(ふくざつ)であるからといって、完璧(かんぺき)にしなくても大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)ですよ。大切(たいせつ)なのは故人(こじん)とご遺族(いぞく)への敬意(けいい)を示(しめ)すことですから。
choices.romaji: Dou itashimashite. Manaa ga fukuzatsu de aru kara to itte, kanpeki ni shinakute mo daijoubu desu yo. Taisetsu na no wa kojin to go-izoku e no keii wo shimesu koto desu kara.
choices.arti: You're welcome. Although the etiquette is complex, it doesn't have to be perfect. What's important is thể hiện respect to the deceased and their family.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Although the etiquette is complex, it doesn't have to be perfect. What's important is thể hiện respect to the deceased and their family' vì sử dụng ~からといって để nhấn mạnh that complexity isn't a reason for excessive worry, and delivers a meaningful core message. Trong khi đó, 'You'll be fine. It's easy' xem nhẹ the seriousness of the topic.

title: Đàm phán hợp đồng kinh doanh

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (Manajer)

question.jepang: 今回の契約(けいやく)の条件(じょうけん)について、御社(おんしゃ)のご意見(いけん)をお聞(き)かせいただけますか。
question.romaji: Konkai no keiyaku no jouken ni tsuite, onsha no go-iken o okikase itadakemasu ka.
question.arti: May I hear your company's opinion regarding the contract terms this time?

choices.jepang: はい、提示(ていじ)いただいた条件(じょうけん)を踏(ふ)まえて、いくつか修正(しゅうせい)をお願(ねが)いしたい点(てん)がございます。
choices.romaji: Hai, teiji itadaita jouken o fumaete, ikutsu ka shuusei o onegai shitai ten ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Yes, based on the proposed terms, there are several points we would like để yêu cầu revisions on.

choices.jepang: 条件(じょうけん)は全部(ぜんぶ)いいです。問題(もんだい)ないと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Jouken wa zenbu ii desu. Mondai nai to omoimasu.
choices.arti: All the terms are fine. I don't think there are any issues.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Based on the proposed terms, there are several points we would like để yêu cầu revisions on' vì sử dụng ~を踏まえて phù hợp và politely in formal business negotiation. Trong khi đó, 'All the terms are fine. I don't think there are any issues' is quá suồng sã, doesn't use keigo, and is không thực tế in a contract negotiation context.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (Direktur)

question.jepang: 納品(のうひん)スケジュールの変更(へんこう)に伴(ともな)い、追加(ついか)コストが発生(はっせい)する可能性(かのうせい)がございます。
question.romaji: Nouhin sukejuuru no henkou ni tomonai, tsuika kosuto ga hassei suru kanousei ga gozaimasu.
question.arti: Along with the change in the delivery schedule, additional costs may arise.

choices.jepang: そのコストはこちらで負担(ふたん)します。心配(しんぱい)しないでください。
choices.romaji: Sono kosuto wa kochira de futan shimasu. Shinpai shinai de kudasai.
choices.arti: We will bear that cost. Don't worry.

choices.jepang: 承知(しょうち)いたしました。追加(ついか)コストの詳細(しょうさい)につきましては、書面(しょめん)にてご提示(ていじ)いただけますでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Shouchi itashimashita. Tsuika kosuto no shousai ni tsukimashite wa, shomen nite go-teiji itadakemasu deshou ka.
choices.arti: I understand. Could the details of the additional costs be provided in writing?

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I understand. Could the details of the additional costs be provided in writing?' vì sử dụng keigo cao (承知いたしました, いただけますでしょうか) and requests written confirmation, which is a chuyên nghiệp business practice. Trong khi đó, 'We will bear that cost. Don't worry' agrees too quickly mà không requesting details, and the tone is too informal for business negotiation.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (Manajer)

question.jepang: 品質(ひんしつ)基準(きじゅん)を引(ひ)き上(あ)げる一方(いっぽう)で、コストを抑(おさ)えることは難(むずか)しいとお考(かんが)えでしょうか。
question.romaji: Hinshitsu kijun o hikia geru ippou de, kosuto o osaeru koto wa muzukashii to o-kangae deshou ka.
question.arti: Do you think that on one hand raising quality standards while on the other hand cutting costs is difficult?

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りで、両立(りょうりつ)は容易(ようい)ではございませんが、最善(さいぜん)の方法(ほうほう)を模索(もさく)したいと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori de, ryouritsu wa youi de wa gozaimasen ga, saizen no houhou o mosaku shitai to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: As you say, balancing both is not easy, but I would like to find the best way.

choices.jepang: そんなこと、やってみればわかります。難(むずか)しくないです。
choices.romaji: Sonna koto, yatte mireba wakarimasu. Muzukashiku nai desu.
choices.arti: Something like that, you'll know once you try. It's not difficult.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'As you say, balancing both is not easy, but I would like to find the best way' vì sử dụng ~と存じます (keigo form of と思う) and thừa nhận difficulty politely trong khi vẫn positive. Trong khi đó, 'Something like that, you'll know once you try. It's not difficult' sounds dismissive and thiếu lịch sự in a formal business negotiation context.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (Direktur)

question.jepang: 支払(しはら)い条件(じょうけん)についてですが、弊社(へいしゃ)としましては、納品(のうひん)後(ご)30日以内(にちいない)のお支払(しはら)いをお願(ねが)いしたいのですが。
question.romaji: Shiharai jouken ni tsuite desu ga, heisha to shimashite wa, nouhin go sanjuunichi inai no o-shiharai wo o-negai shitai no desu ga.
question.arti: Regarding the payment terms, our side would like để yêu cầu payment within 30 days after delivery.

choices.jepang: 30日(にち)は短(みじか)すぎます。60日(にち)にしてください。
choices.romaji: Sanjuunichi wa mijikasugimasu. Rokujuunichi ni shite kudasai.
choices.arti: 30 days is too short. Make it 60 days.

choices.jepang: ご提案(ていあん)は承(うけたまわ)りました。弊社(へいしゃ)の経理(けいり)部門(ぶもん)と協議(きょうぎ)の上(うえ)、可能(かのう)な支払(しはら)い条件(じょうけん)についてご回答(かいとう)申(もう)し上(あ)げたいと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Go-teian wa uketamawarimashita. Heisha no keiri bumon to kyougi no ue, kanou na shiharai jouken ni tsuite go-kaitou moushiagetai to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: We have received your proposal. After discussing with our company's finance department, we would like để cung cấp a response regarding feasible payment terms.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'We have received your proposal. After discussing with our finance department, we would like để cung cấp a response regarding feasible payment terms' vì sử dụng the keigo cao nhất (承りました, 申し上げたい, 存じます) and cho thấy chuyên nghiệp internal processes. Trong khi đó, '30 days is too short. Make it 60 days' is quá trực tiếp and thiếu lịch sự in Japanese business negotiation.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (Manajer)

question.jepang: 今後(こんご)の取引(とりひき)条件(じょうけん)が整(ととの)い次第(しだい)、正式(せいしき)な契約書(けいやくしょ)を作成(さくせい)いたします。
question.romaji: Kongo no torihiki jouken ga totonoi shidai, seishiki na keiyakusho o sakusei itashimasu.
question.arti: Once the terms of future transactions are finalized, we will create a formal contract.

choices.jepang: かしこまりました。契約書(けいやくしょ)の内容(ないよう)につきましては、弊社(へいしゃ)の法務部(ほうむぶ)とも確認(かくにん)の上(うえ)、ご返答(へんとう)申(もう)し上(あ)げます。
choices.romaji: Kashikomarimashita. Keiyakusho no naiyou ni tsukimashite wa, heisha no houmubu tomo kakunin no ue, go-hentou moushiagemasu.
choices.arti: Understood. Regarding the contract contents, after confirming with our legal department as well, we will provide a response.

choices.jepang: 早(はや)くしてください。待(ま)てません。
choices.romaji: Hayaku shite kudasai. Matemasen.
choices.arti: Please hurry. I can't wait.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Understood. Regarding the contract contents, after confirming with our legal department as well, we will provide a response' vì sử dụng かしこまりました (keigo cao nhất) and cho thấy proper business process involving the legal department. Trong khi đó, 'Please hurry. I can't wait' is very thiếu lịch sự and không chuyên nghiệp in a contract negotiation context.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (Direktur)

question.jepang: 守秘(しゅひ)義務(ぎむ)条項(じょうこう)についてですが、契約(けいやく)終了(しゅうりょう)後(ご)も3年間(ねんかん)は有効(ゆうこう)とさせていただきたいのですが。
question.romaji: Shuhi gimu joukou ni tsuite desu ga, keiyaku shuuryou go mo sannenkan wa yuukou to sasete itadakitai no desu ga.
question.arti: Regarding the confidentiality clause, we would like it to remain in effect for 3 years after the contract ends.

choices.jepang: 3年(ねん)は長(なが)すぎます。1年(ねん)にしてください。
choices.romaji: Sannen wa nagasugimasu. Ichinen ni shite kudasai.
choices.arti: 3 years is too long. Make it 1 year.

choices.jepang: 守秘(しゅひ)義務(ぎむ)の重要性(じゅうようせい)は十分(じゅうぶん)に理解(りかい)しております。ただし、3年間(ねんかん)という期間(きかん)については、業界(ぎょうかい)の慣行(かんこう)に基(もと)づいて再検討(さいけんとう)していただくことは可能(かのう)でしょうか。
choices.romaji: Shuhi gimu no juuyousei wa juubun ni rikai shite orimasu. Tadashi, sannenkan to iu kikan ni tsuite wa, gyoukai no kankou ni motozuite saikentou shite itadaku koto wa kanou deshou ka.
choices.arti: We fully understand tầm quan trọng của confidentiality obligations. However, regarding the 3-year period, would it be possible to review it based on industry practices?

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'We understand tầm quan trọng của confidentiality. However, would it be possible to review it based on industry practices?' vì sử dụng ~に基づいて để tham chiếu to objective standards and requests a review diplomatically. Trong khi đó, '3 years is too long. Make it 1 year' is quá trực tiếp and doesn't show understanding of business confidentiality.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (Manajer)

question.jepang: 市場(しじょう)の状況(じょうきょう)にもかかわらず、御社(おんしゃ)との長期(ちょうき)的(てき)なパートナーシップを維持(いじ)したいと考(かんが)えております。
question.romaji: Shijou no joukyou ni mo kakawarazu, onsha to no chouki-teki na paatnaashippu o iji shitai to kangaete orimasu.
question.arti: Despite the current market conditions, we would like to maintain a long-term partnership with your company.

choices.jepang: そうですね。でも、条件(じょうけん)が悪(わる)かったら、やめるかもしれません。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ne. Demo, jouken ga warukattara, yameru kamoshiremasen.
choices.arti: I see. But if the terms are bad, we might stop.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。弊社(へいしゃ)といたしましても、互(たが)いに利益(りえき)を得(え)られる関係(かんけい)を築(きず)いていきたいと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Heisha to itashimashite mo, tagai ni rieki o erareru kankei o kizkuite ikitai to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. Our company also wishes để xây dựng a mutually beneficial relationship.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Our company also wishes để xây dựng a mutually beneficial relationship' vì sử dụng ~といたしましても (keigo form of ~としても) and ~と存じます để đáp lại very chuyên nghiệply. Trong khi đó, 'But if the terms are bad, we might stop' is undiplom atic and could damage long-term business relationships.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (Direktur)

question.jepang: 契約(けいやく)の更新(こうしん)条件(じょうけん)についても、この場(ば)でお話(はなし)しておきたいのですが、よろしいでしょうか。
question.romaji: Keiyaku no koushin jouken ni tsuite mo, kono ba de o-hanashi shite okitai no desu ga, yoroshii deshou ka.
question.arti: Regarding the contract renewal terms, I would also like to discuss them at this opportunity. Would that be agreeable?

choices.jepang: もちろんでございます。契約(けいやく)更新(こうしん)の際(さい)に双方(そうほう)が納得(なっとく)できる条件(じょうけん)を、今(いま)のうちに明確(めいかく)にしておくことが望(のぞ)ましいと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Mochiron de gozaimasu. Keiyaku koushin no sai ni souhou ga nattoku dekiru jouken wo, ima no uchi ni meikaku ni shite oku koto ga nozomashii to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Of course. I think we should clarify the terms acceptable to both parties during renewal from now.

choices.jepang: 更新(こうしん)のことはまた今度(こんど)でいいです。今日(きょう)は時間(じかん)がないので。
choices.romaji: Koushin no koto wa mata kondo de ii desu. Kyou wa jikan ga nai node.
choices.arti: Renewal can be discussed another time. There's no time today.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Of course. We should clarify the terms acceptable to both parties from now' vì cho thấy readiness and chuyên nghiệpism. Trong khi đó, 'Renewal can be discussed another time' misses an important opportunity to clarify terms early.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka (Manajer)

question.jepang: このまま交渉(こうしょう)を続(つづ)けなければ、競合他社(きょうごうたしゃ)との契約(けいやく)を検討(けんとう)せざるを得(え)ない状況(じょうきょう)になりかねません。
question.romaji: Kono mama koushou o tsuzukenakereba, kyougou tasha to no keiyaku o kentou sezaru o enai joukyou ni nari kanemansen.
question.arti: If negotiations continue like this, we may have no choice but to consider a contract with a competing company.

choices.jepang: 他社(たしゃ)と契約(けいやく)すればいいじゃないですか。うちは困(こま)りません。
choices.romaji: Tasha to keiyaku sureba ii ja nai desu ka. Uchi wa komarimasen.
choices.arti: You may contract with another company. We don't mind.

choices.jepang: ご懸念(けねん)はよく理解(りかい)できます。本日中(ほんじつちゅう)に社内(しゃない)で協議(きょうぎ)の上(うえ)、最終的(さいしゅうてき)なご回答(かいとう)をお伝(つた)えできるよう努(つと)めます。
choices.romaji: Go-kenen wa yoku rikai dekimasu. Honjitsu-juu ni shanai de kyougi no ue, saishuuteki na go-kaitou o otsutae dekiru you tsutomemasu.
choices.arti: I fully understand your concern. We will make every effort để cung cấp a final response after internal discussions today.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I fully understand your concern. We will make every effort để cung cấp a final response after internal discussions today' vì chuyên nghiệply responds to subtle pressure by acknowledging the situation and offering a concrete solution with a deadline. Trong khi đó, 'You may contract with another company. We don't mind' is defensive and could damage the business relationship.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto (Direktur)

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)は長時間(ちょうじかん)にわたり、有意義(ゆういぎ)な交渉(こうしょう)ができたと思(おも)います。今後(こんご)ともよろしくお願(ねが)いいたします。
question.romaji: Honjitsu wa choujikan ni watari, yuuigi na koushou ga dekita to omoimasu. Kongo tomo yoroshiku onegai itashimasu.
question.arti: Today I think we were able to have meaningful negotiations over this long time. We look forward to your continued cooperation.

choices.jepang: お疲(つか)れ様(さま)でした。じゃあ、また。
choices.romaji: O-tsukare-sama deshita. Jaa, mata.
choices.arti: Thank you for your hard work. See you.

choices.jepang: こちらこそ、お忙(いそが)しい中(なか)お時間(じかん)をいただき、誠(まこと)にありがとうございました。合意(ごうい)事項(じこう)を書面(しょめん)にまとめ次第(しだい)、改(あらた)めてお送(おく)りいたします。
choices.romaji: Kochira koso, o-isogashii naka o-jikan wo itadaki, makoto ni arigatou gozaimashita. Goui jikou wo shomen ni matome shidai, aratamete o-okuri itashimasu.
choices.arti: It is we who should thank you for your time during your busy schedule. Once the agreement results are compiled in writing, we will send them back.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'It is we who should thank you. Once the agreement results are compiled in writing, we will send them back' vì sử dụng ~次第 and keigo cao for a chuyên nghiệp closing. Trong khi đó, 'Thank you for your hard work. See you' is quá suồng sã for closing a formal business negotiation.

title: Đăng ký nhập học đại học

conversation.speaker: Nhan vien tiep nhan

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)はご来校(らいこう)いただきありがとうございます。入学(にゅうがく)志望(しぼう)の学部(がくぶ)はどちらでしょうか。
question.romaji: Honjitsu wa go-raikou itadaki arigatou gozaimasu. Nyuugaku shibou no gakubu wa dochira deshou ka.
question.arti: Thank you for coming to our school today. Which faculty would you like to enter?

choices.jepang: まだ決(き)めてません。どこでもいいです。
choices.romaji: Mada kimete masen. Doko demo ii desu.
choices.arti: Haven't decided yet. Anywhere is fine.

choices.jepang: 工学部(こうがくぶ)を志望(しぼう)しております。特(とく)に情報(じょうほう)工学(こうがく)の分野(ぶんや)に興味(きょうみ)を持(も)っております。
choices.romaji: Kougakubu o shibou shite orimasu. Toku ni jouhou kougaku no bunya ni kyoumi o motte orimasu.
choices.arti: I would like to enter the Faculty of Engineering. I'm particularly interested in the field of computer science.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I would like to enter the Faculty of Engineering. I'm particularly interested in the field of computer science' vì sử dụng ~を志望しております (keigo) and cho thấy clear, specific interest. Trong khi đó, 'Haven't decided yet. Anywhere is fine' cho thấy lack of preparation and motivation, which is very không phù hợp during a university enrollment consultation.

conversation.speaker: Sinh vien tuong lai

question.jepang: 入学(にゅうがく)試験(しけん)の結果(けっか)を踏(ふ)まえて、奨学金(しょうがくきん)の申請(しんせい)は可能(かのう)でしょうか。
question.romaji: Nyuugaku shiken no kekka o fumaete, shougakukin no shinsei wa kanou deshou ka.
question.arti: Based on the entrance exam results, is scholarship application possible?

choices.jepang: はい、試験(しけん)結果(けっか)が基準(きじゅん)点(てん)を超(こ)えた場合(ばあい)、奨学金(しょうがくきん)委員会(いいんかい)への申請(しんせい)が可能(かのう)となっております。
choices.romaji: Hai, shiken kekka ga kijunten o koeta baai, shougakukin iinkai e no shinsei ga kanou to natte orimasu.
choices.arti: Yes, if the exam results exceed the standard score, application to the scholarship committee can be made.

choices.jepang: お金(かね)が足(た)りないから奨学金(しょうがくきん)が欲(ほ)しいんです。
choices.romaji: O-kane ga tarinai kara shougakukin ga hoshii n desu.
choices.arti: I don't have enough money, so I want a scholarship.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'If the exam results exceed the standard score, application to the scholarship committee can be made' vì cung cấp clear information using ~となっております (keigo for stating regulations). Trong khi đó, 'I don't have enough money, so I want a scholarship' is a response from the wrong perspective — this question is answered by the staff, not the student.

conversation.speaker: Nhan vien tiep nhan

question.jepang: 近年(きんねん)、応募者(おうぼしゃ)数(すう)が増(ふ)えつつある中(なか)で、競争率(きょうそうりつ)も高(たか)まっております。
question.romaji: Kinnen, oubosha suu ga fuetsutsu aru naka de, kyousouritsu mo takamatte orimasu.
question.arti: In recent years, as the number of applicants continues to increase, the competition rate is getting higher.

choices.jepang: そうですか。それでも挑戦(ちょうせん)せざるを得(え)ません。準備(じゅんび)はしっかりしてまいりました。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ka. Soredemo chousen sezaru o emasen. Junbi wa shikkari shite mairimashita.
choices.arti: I see. Even so, I have no choice but to try. I have been preparing myself earnestly.

choices.jepang: じゃあ、受(う)けるのをやめようかな。難(むずか)しそうだし。
choices.romaji: Jaa, ukeru no o yameyou ka na. Muzukashisou da shi.
choices.arti: In that case, maybe I should just give up applying. It looks difficult.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Even so, I have no choice but to try. I have been preparing myself earnestly' vì sử dụng ~ざるを得ない để thể hiện strong determination and ~てまいりました (keigo form of ~てきた). Trong khi đó, 'Maybe I should just give up applying. It looks difficult' cho thấy a defeatist attitude không phù hợp for an enrollment consultation.

conversation.speaker: Sinh vien tuong lai

question.jepang: 入試(にゅうし)の形式(けいしき)について教(おし)えていただけますか。一般(いっぱん)入試(にゅうし)をはじめ、推薦(すいせん)入試(にゅうし)もございますか。
question.romaji: Nyuushi no keishiki ni tsuite oshiete itadakemasu ka. Ippan nyuushi wo hajime, suisen nyuushi mo gozaimasu ka.
question.arti: Could you explain about the entrance exam formats? Starting with the general exam, are there also recommendation exams?

choices.jepang: はい、一般(いっぱん)入試(にゅうし)をはじめ、AO入試(にゅうし)や指定校(していこう)推薦(すいせん)など、複数(ふくすう)の入試(にゅうし)形式(けいしき)をご用意(ようい)しております。ご自身(じしん)の強(つよ)みを活(い)かせる形式(けいしき)をお選(えら)びいただけます。
choices.romaji: Hai, ippan nyuushi wo hajime, AO nyuushi ya shitekou suisen nado, fukusuu no nyuushi keishiki wo go-youi shite orimasu. Go-jishin no tsuyomi wo ikaseru keishiki wo o-erabi itadakemasu.
choices.arti: Yes, starting with the general exam, AO exam and school recommendation, we offer several entrance exam formats. You can choose the format that best utilizes your strengths.

choices.jepang: 入試(にゅうし)のことはウェブサイトで調(しら)べてください。ここでは説明(せつめい)できません。
choices.romaji: Nyuushi no koto wa webu saito de shirabete kudasai. Koko de wa setsumei dekimasen.
choices.arti: Please look up the entrance exam on the website. We can't explain it here.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Starting with the general exam, AO exam and school recommendation, we offer several entrance exam formats' vì sử dụng ~をはじめ phù hợp và cung cấp complete information. Trong khi đó, 'Please look up the entrance exam on the website' is very không chuyên nghiệp for an enrollment consultation staff.

conversation.speaker: Sinh vien tuong lai

question.jepang: 出願(しゅつがん)書類(しょるい)が揃(そろ)い次第(しだい)、提出(ていしゅつ)すればよろしいでしょうか。
question.romaji: Shutsugan shorui ga soroishidai, teishutsu sureba yoroshii deshou ka.
question.arti: May I submit the documents as soon as all enrollment documents are gathered?

choices.jepang: 書類(しょるい)はまとめて出(だ)せばいいんですか。
choices.romaji: Shorui wa matomete daseba ii n desu ka.
choices.arti: Can I just submit the documents all at once?

choices.jepang: はい、書類(しょるい)が揃(そろ)いましたら、受付(うけつけ)窓口(まどぐち)までお持(も)ちいただくか、郵送(ゆうそう)にてお送(おく)りください。締切日(しめきりび)にはご注意(ちゅうい)ください。
choices.romaji: Hai, shorui ga sororimashitara, uketsuke madoguchi made omochi itadaku ka, yuusou nite ookuri kudasai. Shimekiri-bi ni wa go-chuui kudasai.
choices.arti: Yes, once the documents are complete, please bring them to the reception counter or send them by mail. Please note the submission deadline.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Once the documents are complete, please bring them to the reception counter or send them by mail. Please note the submission deadline' vì cung cấp clear and complete instructions using keigo. Trong khi đó, 'Can I just submit the documents all at once?' simply repeats the same question differently and doesn't use appropriate keigo.

conversation.speaker: Nhan vien tiep nhan

question.jepang: 入学(にゅうがく)後(ご)は、勉強(べんきょう)に専念(せんねん)する一方(いっぽう)で、課外(かがい)活動(かつどう)にも積極的(せっきょくてき)に参加(さんか)することをお勧(すす)めしております。
question.romaji: Nyuugaku-go wa, benkyou ni sennen suru ippou de, kagai katsudou ni mo sekkyokuteki ni sanka suru koto o o-susume shite orimasu.
question.arti: After enrollment, we recommend that on one hand you study diligently, but on the other hand also actively participate in extracurricular activities.

choices.jepang: ご助言(じょげん)ありがとうございます。学業(がくぎょう)と課外(かがい)活動(かつどう)のバランスを保(たも)ちながら、充実(じゅうじつ)した学生(がくせい)生活(せいかつ)を送(おく)りたいと思(おも)っております。
choices.romaji: Go-jogen arigatou gozaimasu. Gakugyou to kagai katsudou no baransu o tamochinagara, juujitsu shita gakusei seikatsu o okuritai to omotte orimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you for the advice. While maintaining a balance between academics and extracurricular activities, I want to lead a meaningful student life.

choices.jepang: 課外(かがい)活動(かつどう)は面倒(めんどう)くさいから、勉強(べんきょう)だけします。
choices.romaji: Kagai katsudou wa mendoukusai kara, benkyou dake shimasu.
choices.arti: Extracurricular activities are troublesome, so I'll only study.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Thank you for the advice. While maintaining a balance between academics and extracurricular activities, I want to lead a meaningful student life' vì responds to the advice politely and cho thấy understanding of the concept of ~一方で. Trong khi đó, 'Extracurricular activities are troublesome, so I'll only study' is thiếu lịch sự and cho thấy a negative attitude toward the staff's advice.

conversation.speaker: Sinh vien tuong lai

question.jepang: 学費(がくひ)の分割(ぶんかつ)払(ばら)いは可能(かのう)でしょうか。経済的(けいざいてき)な負担(ふたん)が大(おお)きいものの、ぜひ入学(にゅうがく)したいのですが。
question.romaji: Gakuhi no bunkatsu barai wa kanou deshou ka. Keizaiteki na futan ga ooki mono no, zehi nyuugaku shitai no desu ga.
question.arti: Can tuition be paid in installments? Although the financial burden is large, I really want to enroll.

choices.jepang: 分割(ぶんかつ)払(ばら)いはできません。一括(いっかつ)払(ばら)いのみです。
choices.romaji: Bunkatsu barai wa dekimasen. Ikkatsu barai nomi desu.
choices.arti: Installments are not possible. Payment is lump sum only.

choices.jepang: はい、学費(がくひ)の分割(ぶんかつ)払(ばら)いは可能(かのう)でございます。また、経済的(けいざいてき)なご事情(じじょう)がある場合(ばあい)は、学費(がくひ)減免(げんめん)制度(せいど)もございますので、併(あわ)せてご検討(けんとう)ください。
choices.romaji: Hai, gakuhi no bunkatsu barai wa kanou de gozaimasu. Mata, keizaiteki na go-jijou ga aru baai wa, gakuhi genmen seido mo gozaimasu node, awasete go-kentou kudasai.
choices.arti: Yes, installment payment of tuition is possible. Additionally, if there are certain economic circumstances, there is also a tuition reduction system, so please consider both options.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Yes, installment payment is possible. Additionally, there is also a tuition reduction system' vì cung cấp complete information and cho thấy a helpful attitude. Trong khi đó, 'Installments are not possible. Payment is lump sum only' cung cấp unhelpful information mà không offering alternatives.

conversation.speaker: Nhan vien tiep nhan

question.jepang: 留学(りゅうがく)プログラムへの参加(さんか)は、入学(にゅうがく)後(ご)どのくらいから可能(かのう)になりますでしょうか。
question.romaji: Ryuugaku purогурamu e no sanka wa, nyuugaku-go dono kurai kara kanou ni narimasu deshou ka.
question.arti: Approximately how long after enrollment can participation in exchange programs begin?

choices.jepang: 通常(つうじょう)、入学(にゅうがく)後(ご)2年(ねん)目(め)以降(いこう)から申請(しんせい)可能(かのう)となっており、学業(がくぎょう)成績(せいせき)の基準(きじゅん)を満(み)たす必要(ひつよう)がございます。
choices.romaji: Tsuujou, nyuugaku-go 2-nenme ikou kara shinsei kanou to natte ori, gakugyou seiseki no kijun o mitasu hitsuyou ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Usually, application can be made from the second year after enrollment, and meeting academic grade standards is required.

choices.jepang: 留学(りゅうがく)したいなら、自分(じぶん)で調(しら)べてください。
choices.romaji: Ryuugaku shitai nara, jibun de shirabete kudasai.
choices.arti: If you want exchange programs, look it up yourself.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Usually, application can be made from the second year after enrollment, and meeting academic grade standards is required' vì cung cấp complete and useful information using keigo (~となっており, ~がございます). Trong khi đó, 'If you want exchange programs, look it up yourself' is very thiếu lịch sự and không chuyên nghiệp for a service staff member.

conversation.speaker: Sinh vien tuong lai

question.jepang: 卒業後(そつぎょうご)の就職(しゅうしょく)支援(しえん)制度(せいど)についても伺(うかが)いたいのですが、どのようなサポートがございますか。
question.romaji: Sotsugyou-go no shuushoku shien seido ni tsuite mo ukagaitai no desu ga, dono you na sapooto ga gozaimasu ka.
question.arti: I'd also like để hỏi about the job hunting support system after graduation. What kind of support is available?

choices.jepang: 就職(しゅうしょく)は自分(じぶん)の力(ちから)で見(み)つけるものです。大学(だいがく)に頼(たよ)らないでください。
choices.romaji: Shuushoku wa jibun no chikara de mitsukeru mono desu. Daigaku ni tayoranaide kudasai.
choices.arti: Jobs are found through your own effort. Don't rely on the university.

choices.jepang: キャリアセンターをはじめ、OB・OG訪問(ほうもん)の斡旋(あっせん)やインターンシッププログラムなど、多岐(たき)にわたる支援(しえん)をご用意(ようい)しております。卒業生(そつぎょうせい)の就職率(しゅうしょくりつ)は95%以上(いじょう)を維持(いじ)しております。
choices.romaji: Kyaria sentaa wo hajime, OB OG houmon no assen ya intaanshippu puroguramu nado, taki ni wataru shien wo go-youi shite orimasu. Sotsugyousei no shuushokuritsu wa kyuujuugo paasento ijou wo iji shite orimasu.
choices.arti: Starting with the career center, alumni visit programs, and internship programs, we provide various types of support. Our graduate employment rate is maintained above 95%.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Starting with the career center, alumni visit programs, and internship programs, we provide various types of support' vì sử dụng ~をはじめ and cung cấp convincing concrete data (95%). Trong khi đó, 'Jobs are found through your own effort' is unhelpful and doesn't match the role of a university staff member.

conversation.speaker: Sinh vien tuong lai

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)は詳(くわ)しくご説明(せつめい)いただき、誠(まこと)にありがとうございました。ぜひこちらの大学(だいがく)を受験(じゅけん)したいと思(おも)います。
question.romaji: Honjitsu wa kuwashiku go-setsumei itadaki, makoto ni arigatou gozaimashita. Zehi kochira no daigaku wo juken shitai to omoimasu.
question.arti: Thank you so much for the detailed explanation today. I definitely want to take this university's entrance exam.

choices.jepang: 頑張(がんば)ってね。受(う)かるかどうかはわかりませんけど。
choices.romaji: Ganbatte ne. Ukaru ka dou ka wa wakarimasen kedo.
choices.arti: Good luck. Though whether you pass or not is uncertain.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。ご不明(ふめい)な点(てん)がございましたら、いつでもお問(と)い合(あ)わせください。ご入学(にゅうがく)をお待(ま)ちしております。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Go-fumei na ten ga gozaimashitara, itsu demo o-toiawase kudasai. Go-nyuugaku wo o-machi shite orimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. If anything is unclear, please contact us anytime. We look forward to your application.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Thank you. If anything is unclear, please contact us anytime. We look forward to your application' vì cung cấp a warm and chuyên nghiệp closing. Trong khi đó, 'Good luck. Though whether you pass or not is uncertain' is very không chuyên nghiệp and could lower the prospective student's motivation.

title: Tư vấn y tế nghiêm trọng

conversation.speaker: Bac si

question.jepang: 検査(けんさ)結果(けっか)を踏(ふ)まえて、現在(げんざい)の状態(じょうたい)についてご説明(せつめい)いたします。
question.romaji: Kensa kekka o fumaete, genzai no joutai ni tsuite go-setsumei itashimasu.
question.arti: Based on the examination results, I will explain your current condition.

choices.jepang: はい、よく聞(き)かせていただきます。何(なに)か深刻(しんこく)な問題(もんだい)がございますでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Hai, yoku kikasete itadakimasu. Nani ka shinkou na mondai ga gozaimasu deshou ka.
choices.arti: Yes, I will listen carefully. Is there a serious problem?

choices.jepang: はい、先生(せんせい)のご説明(せつめい)を伺(うかが)いたいと思(おも)います。どうぞよろしくお願(ねが)いいたします。
choices.romaji: Hai, sensei no go-setsumei o ukagaitai to omoimasu. Douzo yoroshiku onegai itashimasu.
choices.arti: Yes, I would like to hear the doctor's explanation. I'm in your care.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I will listen carefully. Is there a serious problem?' vì cho thấy readiness to listen while actively asking relevant questions in a serious medical consultation. Trong khi đó, 'I would like to hear the doctor's explanation. I'm in your care' is polite but quá thụ động and doesn't show initiative.

conversation.speaker: Bac si

question.jepang: 症状(しょうじょう)が悪化(あっか)しつつある状況(じょうきょう)にもかかわらず、治療(ちりょう)を始(はじ)めるのが遅(おそ)くなってしまいました。
question.romaji: Shoujou ga akka shitsutsu aru joukyou ni mo kakawarazu, chiryou o hajimeru no ga osoku natte shimaimashita.
question.arti: Despite the symptoms continuing to worsen, starting treatment was delayed.

choices.jepang: 先生(せんせい)のせいですか？早(はや)く言(い)ってくれればよかったのに。
choices.romaji: Sensei no sei desu ka? Hayaku itte kurereba yokatta no ni.
choices.arti: Is that the doctor's fault? You should have said something sooner.

choices.jepang: それは申(もう)し訳(わけ)ございませんでした。症状(しょうじょう)を軽(かる)く見(み)ておりました。今後(こんご)の治療(ちりょう)方針(ほうしん)についてお聞(き)かせいただけますか。
choices.romaji: Sore wa moushiwake gozaimasen deshita. Shoujou o karuku mite orimashita. Kongo no chiryou houshin ni tsuite okikase itadakemasu ka.
choices.arti: I'm truly sorry. I underestimated the symptoms. May I hear about the treatment plan going forward?

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I'm truly sorry. I underestimated the symptoms. May I hear about the treatment plan going forward?' vì takes responsibility for the delay and mang tính xây dựngly asks about next steps. Trong khi đó, 'Is that the doctor's fault? You should have said something sooner' rudely blames the doctor.

conversation.speaker: Benh nhan

question.jepang: 手術(しゅじゅつ)を受(う)けないと、症状(しょうじょう)がさらに悪(あく)化(か)しかねないということでしょうか。
question.romaji: Shujutsu o ukenai to, shoujou ga sara ni akka shikanenai to iu koto deshou ka.
question.arti: Does this mean that mà không surgery, the symptoms could worsen further?

choices.jepang: まあ、大丈夫(だいじょうぶ)だと思(おも)います。心配(しんぱい)しすぎですよ。
choices.romaji: Maa, daijoubu da to omoimasu. Shinpai shisugimasu yo.
choices.arti: Yes, I think it's fine. You're worrying too much.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。手術(しゅじゅつ)を受(う)けずに放置(ほうち)した場合(ばあい)、重篤(じゅうとく)な合併症(がっぺいしょう)を引(ひ)き起(お)こしかねません。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Shujutsu o kezuni houchi shita baai, juutoku na gappei shou o hikiokoushi kanemansen.
choices.arti: That is correct. If left mà không surgery, it could lead to serious complications.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'That is correct. If left mà không surgery, it could lead to serious complications' vì sử dụng ~かねません để mô tả medical risk chuyên nghiệply. Trong khi đó, 'I think it's fine. You're worrying too much' xem nhẹ a serious medical condition.

conversation.speaker: Bac si

question.jepang: 患者(かんじゃ)様(さま)の年齢(ねんれい)や体力(たいりょく)に伴(ともな)い、手術(しゅじゅつ)のリスクも考慮(こうりょ)する必要(ひつよう)がございます。
question.romaji: Kanja-sama no nenrei ya tairyoku ni tomonai, shujutsu no risuku mo kouryo suru hitsuyou ga gozaimasu.
question.arti: Along with the patient's age and physical condition, the risks of surgery also need to be considered.

choices.jepang: わかりました。リスクと利点(りてん)を両方(りょうほう)詳(くわ)しく教(おし)えていただき、インフォームドコンセントの上(うえ)で決定(けってい)したいと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Wakarimashita. Risuku to riten o ryouhou kuwashiku oshiete itadaki, infoomu do konsento no ue de kettei shitai to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: I understand. I want to make a decision based on informed consent after knowing the risks and benefits in detail.

choices.jepang: リスクについて、もう少(すこ)し詳(くわ)しくご説明(せつめい)いただけますでしょうか。家族(かぞく)とも相談(そうだん)した上(うえ)で、判断(はんだん)したいと考(かんが)えております。
choices.romaji: Risuku ni tsuite, mou sukoshi kuwashiku go-setsumei itadakemasu deshou ka. Kazoku to mo soudan shita ue de, handan shitai to kangaete orimasu.
choices.arti: Could you explain the risks in more detail? I'd like to make a decision after discussing with my family.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I want to make a decision based on informed consent after knowing the risks and benefits' vì mentions the medically relevant concept of informed consent. Trong khi đó, 'Could you explain more? I'd like to discuss with my family' is polite but doesn't show toàn diện understanding of the medical decision-making process.

conversation.speaker: Benh nhan

question.jepang: 手術(しゅじゅつ)以外(いがい)の治療法(ちりょうほう)はないのでしょうか。できれば手術(しゅじゅつ)を避(さ)けたいのですが。
question.romaji: Shujutsu igai no chiryouhou wa nai no deshou ka. Dekireba shujutsu wo saketai no desu ga.
question.arti: Is there no treatment method other than surgery? If possible, I'd like to avoid surgery.

choices.jepang: お気持(きも)ちはよくわかります。薬物(やくぶつ)療法(りょうほう)という選択肢(せんたくし)もございますが、症状(しょうじょう)の進行(しんこう)具合(ぐあい)を踏(ふ)まえると、手術(しゅじゅつ)が最(もっと)も効果的(こうかてき)であると判断(はんだん)しております。
choices.romaji: O-kimochi wa yoku wakarimasu. Yakubutsu ryouhou to iu sentakushi mo gozaimasu ga, shoujou no shinkou guai wo fumaeru to, shujutsu ga mottomo koukateki de aru to handan shite orimasu.
choices.arti: I fully understand your feelings. There is also the option of drug therapy, but considering the degree of symptom progression, we assess surgery as the most effective.

choices.jepang: 手術(しゅじゅつ)しかありません。他(ほか)の方法(ほうほう)は効(き)きません。
choices.romaji: Shujutsu shika arimasen. Hoka no houhou wa kikimasen.
choices.arti: There's only surgery. Other methods are not effective.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'I understand your feelings. There is also drug therapy, but considering the degree of symptom progression, surgery is the most effective' vì sử dụng ~を踏まえると chuyên nghiệply while thể hiện empathy. Trong khi đó, 'There's only surgery. Other methods are not effective' is too blunt and doesn't provide adequate explanation.

conversation.speaker: Bac si

question.jepang: 手術(しゅじゅつ)を行(おこな)う一方(いっぽう)で、術後(じゅつご)のリハビリも重要(じゅうよう)になってまいります。回復(かいふく)には数(すう)ヶ月(かげつ)かかる見込(みこ)みです。
question.romaji: Shujutsu wo okonau ippou de, jutsugo no rihabiri mo juuyou ni natte mairimasu. Kaifuku ni wa suukagetsu kakaru mikomi desu.
question.arti: On one hand performing the surgery, on the other hand post-operative rehabilitation also becomes important. Recovery is estimated to take several months.

choices.jepang: 承知(しょうち)いたしました。リハビリの内容(ないよう)や頻度(ひんど)について、もう少(すこ)し詳(くわ)しく教(おし)えていただけますか。仕事(しごと)との両立(りょうりつ)についても相談(そうだん)させていただきたいのですが。
choices.romaji: Shouchi itashimashita. Rihabiri no naiyou ya hindo ni tsuite, mou sukoshi kuwashiku oshiete itadakemasu ka. Shigoto to no ryouritsu ni tsuite mo soudan sasete itadakitai no desu ga.
choices.arti: I understand. Could you explain the content and frequency of rehabilitation in more detail? I'd also like to consult about how to balance it with work.

choices.jepang: 数(すう)ヶ月(かげつ)もかかるんですか。仕事(しごと)があるので困(こま)ります。
choices.romaji: Suukagetsu mo kakaru n desu ka. Shigoto ga aru node komarimasu.
choices.arti: Several months? I have work so it's troublesome.

conversation.explanation: Câu trả lời đúng là 'Could you explain the rehabilitation details in more detail? I'd also like to consult about how to balance it with work' vì cho thấy a proactive and practical attitude. Trong khi đó, 'Several months? I have work so it's troublesome' only complains mà không seeking solutions.

conversation.speaker: Benh nhan

question.jepang: 治療(ちりょう)を続(つづ)けつつある中(なか)で、日常(にちじょう)生活(せいかつ)での注意点(ちゅういてん)はありますでしょうか。
question.romaji: Chiryou o tsuzuketsutsu aru naka de, nichijou seikatsu de no chuuiten wa arimasu deshou ka.
question.arti: During the ongoing course of treatment, are there things to be careful about in daily life?

choices.jepang: はい、いくつかございます。食事(しょくじ)制限(せいげん)を守(まも)っていただくとともに、激(はげ)しい運動(うんどう)はお控(ひか)えいただきますよう、お願(ねが)い申(もう)し上(あ)げます。
choices.romaji: Hai, ikutsu ka gozaimasu. Shokuji seigen o mamotte itadaku to tomo ni, hageshii undou wa o-hikae itadakimasu you, onegai moushiagemasu.
choices.arti: Yes, there are several. Please follow dietary restrictions and avoid intense exercise.
